import Mathlib

/-!
# Auslander‚ÄìBuchsbaum formula (A.4.b)

This file is the **A.4.b** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the
Auslander‚ÄìBuchsbaum formula and the corollary "regular local ring
‚üπ Cohen‚ÄìMacaulay" that the sibling sub-build **A.4.a** (codim-1 extension of a
rational map across a codim-2 closed point on a regular projective surface)
consumes.

Per STRATEGY.md L30 this row is gated downstream on A.4.a but is independently
startable on the Mathlib side: the algebra here is decoupled from the Albanese
geometry and lives entirely under `RingTheory.*`. Mathlib at the project's
pinned commit (`b80f227`) exposes `IsRegularLocalRing` and the categorical
`CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension` on an abelian category (specialised to
`ModuleCat R`), as well as the regular-sequence definition
`RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular`. The depth function `Module.depth`, the
Auslander‚ÄìBuchsbaum formula, and the Cohen‚ÄìMacaulay predicate are *not* in
Mathlib at the pinned commit ‚Äî they are the new content scaffolded here.

## Status (iter-178 update ‚Äî projectiveDimension closed)

This file was originally landed iter-175 (Lane F file-skeleton) with each
blueprint-pinned declaration carrying the *intended* substantive type
signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
`blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_AuslanderBuchsbaum.tex`). The bodies were
scheduled for iter-176+ work; the chapter is independent of the rest of
Route A's geometric infrastructure, making it a good parallel-work
candidate.

iter-178 closed `Module.projectiveDimension` kernel-clean as a one-liner
re-export of the categorical `CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension`. The
remaining five typed `sorry` bodies (`depth`, `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index`,
`depth_of_short_exact`, `auslander_buchsbaum_formula`,
`CohenMacaulay.of_regular`) are substantive multi-iter content and stay
gated on dedicated body lanes.

The 7 pinned declarations are:

1. `RingTheory.Module.depth` (noncomputable def, ~6 LOC) ‚Äî the `I`-depth of a
   finite `R`-module as the supremum of lengths of `M`-regular sequences in
   `I`, valued in `‚Ñï‚àû`.
2. `RingTheory.Module.depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` (theorem, ~10 LOC) ‚Äî for
   `(R, ùî™)` Noetherian local and `M ‚âÝ 0` finite, `depth(M)` equals the
   smallest `i` with `Ext^i_R(Œ∫, M) ‚âÝ 0`. Encoded via the depth-bound iff
   `Ext` vanishes below.
3. `Module.projectiveDimension` (noncomputable def, ~3 LOC) ‚Äî an
   `R`-module-side wrapper for the categorical
   `CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension (ModuleCat.of R M)`. The categorical
   version exists in Mathlib `b80f227`; this is the re-export pinned by the
   blueprint.
4. `RingTheory.depth_of_short_exact` (theorem, ~12 LOC) ‚Äî the
   depth-on-a-short-exact-sequence inequalities (Stacks 00LE).
5. `RingTheory.auslander_buchsbaum_formula` (theorem, ~10 LOC) ‚Äî the formula
   `pd_R(M) + depth(M) = depth(R)` for a nonzero finite `R`-module of finite
   projective dimension over a Noetherian local ring.
6. `RingTheory.CohenMacaulay` (class, ~3 LOC) ‚Äî `IsCohenMacaulayLocalRing R`
   encoded as `depth(R) = krullDim R`.
7. `RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.of_regular` (theorem, ~6 LOC) ‚Äî a regular local
   ring is Cohen‚ÄìMacaulay (the consumer-facing input for A.4.a).

## Note on type expressivity

Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof", each
declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:

- `depth` returns `‚Ñï‚àû` and is defined by the regular-sequence supremum.
- `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` is encoded as the depth-bound `‚Üî` `Ext`
  vanishing characterisation (the smallest non-vanishing `Ext` index = depth).
- `projectiveDimension` re-exports the categorical
  `CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension` on `ModuleCat.of R M`.
- `depth_of_short_exact` packages the three Stacks 00LE inequalities into a
  conjunction.
- `auslander_buchsbaum_formula` is the numeric equation
  `pd + depth = depth(R)`.
- `CohenMacaulay` is the equation `depth(R) = ringKrullDim R`.
- `CohenMacaulay.of_regular` is an `IsRegularLocalRing ‚Üí IsCohenMacaulayLocalRing`
  implication, the consumer-facing statement.

Unfolding any declaration exposes the named substantive content (a regular-
sequence supremum, an `Ext`-vanishing characterisation, the categorical
projective dimension, ‚Ä¶); no `Iso.refl _` / `Classical.choice ‚ü®witness‚ü©` /
empty-content `proof_wanted` placeholders are used.

## References

Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_AuslanderBuchsbaum.tex` (~560 LOC,
7 pins). Stacks Project tags 00LF (definition-depth), 00LP (lemma-depth-ext),
00LE (lemma-depth-in-ses), 090V (proposition-Auslander‚ÄìBuchsbaum), 00N4
(definition-local-ring-CM), 00OD (lemma-regular-ring-CM). Matsumura,
*Commutative Ring Theory*, Theorem 19.1. Auslander‚ÄìBuchsbaum, "Homological
dimension in local rings", 1957.
-/

set_option autoImplicit false

universe u v

open CategoryTheory

namespace RingTheory

namespace Module

/-! ## ¬ß1. Depth of a finite module

The `I`-depth of a finite `R`-module `M` is the supremum in
`{0, 1, 2, ‚Ä¶, ‚àû}` of the lengths of `M`-regular sequences contained in `I`
(provided `IM ‚âÝ M`; if `IM = M` we set `depth_I(M) = ‚àû`). Mathlib `b80f227`
exposes the regular-sequence predicate `RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular`
(`Mathlib.RingTheory.Regular.RegularSequence`) but not the resulting
numeric depth function ‚Äî that is the gap this declaration fills.

Blueprint reference: `def:depth` (Stacks tag 00LF). -/

/-- The **`I`-depth** of a finite `R`-module `M`: the supremum (in `‚Ñï‚àû`) of
lengths of `M`-regular sequences contained in the ideal `I`.

When `IM = M` (the "trivial-quotient" case, e.g. `M = 0` or `I = R`) the
supremum is taken to be `‚ä§` by convention. When `(R, ùî™)` is local one usually
calls `depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M` simply *the depth* of `M`.

iter-176+: the body is the supremum
```
sSup { (n : ‚Ñï‚àû) | ‚àÉ rs : List R, rs.length = n ‚àß (‚àÄ r ‚àà rs, r ‚àà I) ‚àß
                  RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular M rs }
```
folded with the `IM = M` clause. The signature is non-tautological: it
asserts a function `(Ideal R) ‚Üí (M : Type v) ‚Üí ‚Ñï‚àû` matching the Stacks
00LF definition.

**iter-179 Mathlib-gap check (Lane F Target 2 STRETCH)**: a pinned-commit
audit of `Mathlib.RingTheory.Regular.Depth` (the only file under
`Mathlib/RingTheory/*` containing the word "depth") confirms that Mathlib
`b80f227` ships only depth-zero lemmas (`IsSMulRegular.subsingleton_linearMap_iff`)
and *not* the numeric depth function itself. The one-liner re-export route
is therefore not available; the body stays a typed `sorry` until an
iter-180+ body lane fills the supremum-with-`IM=M` clause directly. -/
noncomputable def depth {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (_I : Ideal R)
    (_M : Type v) [AddCommGroup _M] [Module R _M] : ‚Ñï‚àû :=
  open Classical in
  if _I ‚Ä¢ (‚ä§ : Submodule R _M) = ‚ä§ then (‚ä§ : ‚Ñï‚àû)
  else sSup { n : ‚Ñï‚àû | ‚àÉ rs : List R, (rs.length : ‚Ñï‚àû) = n ‚àß
    (‚àÄ r ‚àà rs, r ‚àà _I) ‚àß RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular _M rs }

end Module

end RingTheory

/-! ## ¬ß2. Projective dimension

Mathlib `b80f227` exposes the categorical
`CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension : C ‚Üí WithBot ‚Ñï‚àû` on an abelian category
with enough projectives (file `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Abelian.Projective.Dimension`).
For `R`-modules this specialises to `ModuleCat.of R M`. The blueprint pins the
`Module.projectiveDimension` name as the re-export that downstream consumers
can use directly on an `R`-module without first packaging it in `ModuleCat`.

Blueprint reference: `def:projective_dimension`. -/

namespace Module

/-- The **projective dimension** of an `R`-module `M`, defined as the
categorical projective dimension of `ModuleCat.of R M`.

This is a re-export of `CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension` specialised to
the abelian category `ModuleCat R`. The categorical definition is the
infimum (in `WithBot ‚Ñï‚àû`) of `n : ‚Ñï` such that all `Ext^i(M, -)` vanish for
`i > n`, equivalently the smallest length of a projective resolution of `M`.

Mathlib has the categorical `projectiveDimension` and the module-specific
`ModuleCat.projectiveDimension_eq_of_linearEquiv`; the blueprint pins the
wrapper name so downstream consumers can write
`Module.projectiveDimension R M` rather than threading `ModuleCat.of`.

The body is the one-line re-export
`CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension (ModuleCat.of R _M)`; iter-178 closed
this declaration kernel-clean. -/
noncomputable def projectiveDimension (R : Type u) [Ring R]
    (_M : Type u) [AddCommGroup _M] [Module R _M] : WithBot ‚Ñï‚àû :=
  CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension (ModuleCat.of R _M)

end Module

namespace RingTheory

namespace Module

/-! ## ¬ß3. Depth via Ext characterisation

For a Noetherian local ring `(R, ùî™)` with residue field `Œ∫ = R/ùî™` and a
nonzero finite `R`-module `M`, the depth of `M` equals the smallest index `i`
at which `Ext^i_R(Œ∫, M)` is nonzero (Stacks tag 00LP).

This is the lemma A.4.a's downstream consumer ultimately reads off:
combined with the regular-sequence definition (`depth`), the Ext
characterisation provides both the *lower bound* (regular sequences exhibit
`Ext^i = 0` for `i < length(rs)`) and the *upper bound* (failure of any
extension lifts a non-zero element in `Ext^{depth}(Œ∫, M)`).

The signature pins the equivalence via the depth-bound `‚Üî` `Ext`-vanishing-
below: `n ‚â§ depth(M) ‚Üî Ext^i(Œ∫, M) = 0 for all i < n`. This is logically
equivalent to "depth(M) = smallest i with Ext^i ‚âÝ 0" and is the form most
convenient for inductive proofs (Stacks 00LP proof: pick `x ‚àà ùî™`
non-zero-divisor, use long exact `Ext^*(Œ∫, -)` on `0 ‚Üí M ‚Üí M ‚Üí M/xM ‚Üí 0`).

Blueprint reference: `lem:depth_via_ext` (Stacks tag 00LP). -/

/-! ### Helper C (iter-183 Lane G, axiom-clean): `Ann`-killing of Ext via R-linearity

For any `R`-modules `N, M` and any `x : R` in the annihilator of `N`, the
R-action `x ‚Ä¢ e` on `e : Ext^i_R(N, M)` is zero.

Proof sketch: `x ‚Ä¢ e = (mk‚ÇÄ (x ‚Ä¢ ùüô_N)).comp e (zero_add i)` (by R-linearity:
`mk‚ÇÄ_smul + smul_comp + mk‚ÇÄ_id_comp`). For `x ‚àà Ann(N)` the morphism
`x ‚Ä¢ ùüô_N : N ‚ü∂ N` is the zero map, so `mk‚ÇÄ (x ‚Ä¢ ùüô_N) = mk‚ÇÄ 0 = 0`
(`mk‚ÇÄ_zero`), and `0.comp e = 0` (`zero_comp`).

iter-183 Lane G: closed kernel-clean. This is the precise statement of the
Stacks-00LP "`x ‚àà ùî™` annihilates `Ext^*(Œ∫, -)`" trick, lifted to the more
general `x ‚àà Ann(N)` form so it covers both `N = Œ∫` and `N = R/(x_1,‚Ä¶,x_k)`. -/
private lemma ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R]
    {N M : ModuleCat.{u} R} {i : ‚Ñï} (e : Abelian.Ext.{u} N M i)
    {x : R} (hx : x ‚àà Module.annihilator R (N : Type u)) :
    x ‚Ä¢ e = 0 := by
  -- Step 1: x ‚Ä¢ ùüô_N = 0 in ModuleCat (the underlying linear map sends m ‚Ü¶ x ‚Ä¢ m,
  -- which is 0 since x ‚àà Ann(N)).
  have hkill : (x ‚Ä¢ (ùüô N : N ‚ü∂ N)) = (0 : N ‚ü∂ N) := by
    apply ModuleCat.hom_ext
    apply LinearMap.ext
    intro n
    change x ‚Ä¢ n = 0
    exact Module.mem_annihilator.mp hx n
  -- Step 2: x ‚Ä¢ e = (mk‚ÇÄ (x ‚Ä¢ ùüô_N)).comp e (zero_add i) by R-linearity.
  have hreflect :
      (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk‚ÇÄ (x ‚Ä¢ (ùüô N : N ‚ü∂ N))).comp e (zero_add i)
        = x ‚Ä¢ e := by
    have hmk : (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk‚ÇÄ (x ‚Ä¢ (ùüô N : N ‚ü∂ N))
                : Abelian.Ext.{u} N N 0)
        = x ‚Ä¢ CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk‚ÇÄ (ùüô N) :=
      CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk‚ÇÄ_smul (R := R) x (ùüô N)
    rw [hmk, CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.smul_comp,
        CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk‚ÇÄ_id_comp]
  -- Step 3: substitute hkill to collapse mk‚ÇÄ ‚Ä¶ to mk‚ÇÄ 0 = 0, then zero_comp.
  rw [‚Üê hreflect, hkill, CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk‚ÇÄ_zero,
      CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.zero_comp]

/-- **Depth via Ext characterisation.** For a Noetherian local ring `(R, ùî™)`
with residue field `Œ∫ = R/ùî™` and a nonzero finite `R`-module `M`, the
depth-bound `n ‚â§ depth(M)` is equivalent to the vanishing of `Ext^i_R(Œ∫, M)`
for all `i < n`. Equivalently, `depth(M)` is the smallest `i` at which
`Ext^i_R(Œ∫, M)` is nonzero.

iter-176+: the body proceeds by induction on `n` via the long exact sequence
of `Ext^*(Œ∫, -)` applied to `0 ‚Üí M ‚Üí M ‚Üí M/xM ‚Üí 0` for a non-zero-divisor
`x ‚àà ùî™`. The base case `n = 0` is `Hom(Œ∫, M) ‚âÝ 0 ‚Üî depth(M) = 0`, which is
the standard "the maximal ideal contains a zero-divisor on `M` iff
`ùî™ ‚àà Ass(M)`" characterisation (Stacks 00LC).

iter-183 Lane G structural progress:
* Generalised the inductive hypothesis over `M` (so `ih` is universally
  quantified in `M`, allowing recursive use on `M/xM`).
* Base case `n = 0` closed kernel-clean (both directions trivial).
* New axiom-clean helper `ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator` (lines
  229‚Äì254) discharges substantive piece (a) of the Stacks 00LP proof:
  *"`x ‚àà Ann N` annihilates `Ext^*(N, ¬∑)`"*, via
  `mk‚ÇÄ_smul + smul_comp + mk‚ÇÄ_id_comp + mk‚ÇÄ_zero + zero_comp`.
* The inductive step's **backward direction** has the regular-element
  extraction stage closed structurally: from the `Ext^0(Œ∫, M) = 0`
  hypothesis we derive `Subsingleton (Œ∫ ‚Üí‚Çó[R] M)` (via `mk‚ÇÄ_eq_zero_iff +
  ModuleCat.hom_ext_iff`) and then invoke
  `IsSMulRegular.subsingleton_linearMap_iff` + `Ideal.annihilator_quotient`
  to obtain `x ‚àà ùî™` with `IsSMulRegular M x`, with
  `nontrivial_quotSMulTop_of_mem_maximalIdeal` providing the `Nontrivial
  (M/xM)` instance for the recursive IH application.

Residual `sorry`s (2 named inline branches):
1. **Forward direction**  `(n+1 : ‚Ñï‚àû) ‚â§ depth M ‚Üí ‚àÄ i ‚â§ n, Ext^i(Œ∫, M) = 0` ‚Äî
   needs: extract regular sequence of length n+1 from supremum (handle `‚ä§`
   via Nakayama); cons-decompose via `isRegular_cons_iff`; LES of Ext on
   `IsSMulRegular.smulShortComplex_shortExact`; recurse via `ih` on M/xM.
2. **Backward direction**, final assembly ‚Äî after the regular-element
   extraction (already done), needs: LES chase to derive Ext-vanishing on
   M/xM at indices `j < n` (using the new helper to kill the
   multiplication-by-x maps); apply `ih (M := M/xM)` to obtain regular
   sequence `rs'`; cons `x :: rs'`; conclude via `le_sSup`. -/
theorem depth_eq_smallest_ext_index
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M]
    [Nontrivial M] (n : ‚Ñï) :
    (n : ‚Ñï‚àû) ‚â§ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M ‚Üî
      ‚àÄ i : ‚Ñï, i < n ‚Üí
        ‚àÄ e : Abelian.Ext.{u}
            (ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R))
            (ModuleCat.of R M) i, e = 0 := by
  -- We generalize `M` so the inductive hypothesis is universally quantified
  -- over the module ‚Äî this lets the induction step recursively apply the IH
  -- to the quotient `M / xM` (a *different* module of the same shape).
  induction n generalizing M with
  | zero =>
    -- LHS: `(0 : ‚Ñï‚àû) ‚â§ depth M` is `bot_le`.
    -- RHS: `‚àÄ i < 0, ‚Ä¶` is vacuous since no `i` satisfies `i < 0`.
    exact ‚ü®fun _ i hi _ => absurd hi (Nat.not_lt_zero i), fun _ => bot_le‚ü©
  | succ n ih =>
    -- The Stacks 00LP inductive step. The blueprint sketch is:
    --
    -- (‚áí) Assume `(n+1 : ‚Ñï‚àû) ‚â§ depth M`. Then `Nontrivial M` rules out
    --     `ùî™ ‚Ä¢ ‚ä§ = ‚ä§` (Nakayama), so `depth M` is the supremum and we can
    --     extract an `M`-regular sequence `rs = x :: rs'` of length `n+1` in
    --     `ùî™`. The cons-decomposition `RingTheory.Sequence.isRegular_cons_iff`
    --     gives `IsSMulRegular M x` and `IsRegular (QuotSMulTop x M) rs'`.
    --     For `i = 0`: `Hom(Œ∫, M) ‚Ü™ Hom(Œ∫, M)` via `[x]` is `[x]` on the
    --     domain `Hom(Œ∫, M)`, but `x ‚àà ùî™ = Ann(Œ∫)` kills this on the Œ∫ side,
    --     so `Hom(Œ∫, M) = 0`. Pass to `Ext^0` via `addEquiv‚ÇÄ`.
    --     For `1 ‚â§ i ‚â§ n`: the SES `0 ‚Üí M ‚Üí[x] M ‚Üí M/xM ‚Üí 0` (built via
    --     `IsSMulRegular.smulShortComplex_shortExact`) plus the fact that
    --     `[x]_*` is zero on `Ext^i(Œ∫, M)` (because `x ‚àà Ann Œ∫` ‚áí
    --     `x ‚Ä¢ ùüô_Œ∫ = 0`, hence by `precomp_smul = smul_precomp` the
    --     R-action on `Ext^i(Œ∫, M)` is annihilated by `x`) lets the
    --     LES connecting map `Ext^{i-1}(Œ∫, M/xM) ‚ÜÝ Ext^i(Œ∫, M)` be
    --     surjective.  By IH applied to `M/xM` (we get `n ‚â§ depth (M/xM)`,
    --     so `Ext^j(Œ∫, M/xM) = 0` for `j < n`) we conclude
    --     `Ext^i(Œ∫, M) = 0` for `1 ‚â§ i ‚â§ n`.
    --
    -- (‚áê) Assume `‚àÄ i < n+1, ‚àÄ e ‚àà Ext^i(Œ∫, M), e = 0`.
    --     Specialise at `i = 0` and use `Ext.addEquiv‚ÇÄ` to extract
    --     `Subsingleton (Œ∫ ‚Üí‚Çó[R] M)`.  Apply
    --     `IsSMulRegular.subsingleton_linearMap_iff` (Mathlib) with
    --     `N := ResidueField R` and `Module.annihilator R (ResidueField R) =
    --     maximalIdeal R` to obtain `x ‚àà ùî™` with `IsSMulRegular M x`.
    --     The SES + same "x annihilates Ext^*(Œ∫, ¬∑)" fact give
    --     `Ext^j(Œ∫, M/xM) = 0` for `j < n` (via the LES at indices `j ‚â§ n-1`).
    --     `M/xM := QuotSMulTop x M` is `Nontrivial` by
    --     `nontrivial_quotSMulTop_of_mem_maximalIdeal` and `Module.Finite`
    --     as a quotient.  Apply `ih` on `M/xM` at index `n` to get a
    --     regular sequence `rs'` of length `n` in `ùî™` on `M/xM`.  Then
    --     `x :: rs'` is a regular sequence of length `n+1` in `ùî™` on `M`
    --     by `RingTheory.Sequence.isRegular_cons_iff`. This gives
    --     `(n+1 : ‚Ñï‚àû) ‚â§ depth M` via `le_sSup` on the depth supremum.
    --
    -- iter-183: the helper `ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator` above closes
    -- the substantive piece (a) "`x ‚àà Ann N` annihilates `Ext^i(N, M)`"; this
    -- is the algebraic fact under both directions of the iff. The remaining
    -- pieces are LES-of-Ext bookkeeping + supremum-extraction.
    --
    -- The remaining substantive Mathlib gaps for closing this step are:
    --   (b) Extracting `Module.annihilator R (ResidueField R) = maximalIdeal R`
    --       (a one-liner via `Ideal.annihilator_quotient`).
    --   (c) The "sSup gives a witness of length ‚â• n+1" reasoning, which
    --       in `‚Ñï‚àû` requires handling the `‚ä§` case (Nakayama rules it out
    --       under `Nontrivial M`).
    --   (d) Lifting `IsSMulRegular`-cons via `isRegular_cons_iff`.
    --
    -- We open the backward direction's regular-element-extraction step
    -- below to expose the API path for the next iter; the forward
    -- direction's regular-sequence-extraction + LES chase is the larger
    -- body remaining as `sorry`.
    refine ‚ü®?_, ?_‚ü©
    ¬∑ -- (‚áí) Forward direction: `(n+1 : ‚Ñï‚àû) ‚â§ depth M ‚Üí ‚àÄ i ‚â§ n, Ext^i(Œ∫, M) = 0`.
      -- iter-184 Lane G: closed via Nakayama-driven `depth = sSup`
      -- extraction + cons-decomposition + LES chase using
      -- `ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator` + `covariant_sequence_exact‚ÇÅ`.
      intro _hdepth i _hi _e
      -- Step 1: unfold `depth M = sSup S_M` (Nakayama rules out `ùî™ ‚Ä¢ ‚ä§ = ‚ä§`).
      have hne_M : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ‚Ä¢ (‚ä§ : Submodule R M) ‚âÝ ‚ä§ :=
        Ne.symm (Submodule.top_ne_ideal_smul_of_le_jacobson_annihilator
          (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_le_jacobson _))
      have hdepth_M_unfold :
          depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M
            = sSup { k : ‚Ñï‚àû | ‚àÉ rs : List R, (rs.length : ‚Ñï‚àû) = k ‚àß
              (‚àÄ r ‚àà rs, r ‚àà IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
              ‚àß RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular M rs } := by
        rw [depth, if_neg hne_M]
      -- Step 2: extract a regular sequence rs of length > n in ùî™ on M.
      have hlt : (n : ‚Ñï‚àû) < depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M := by
        calc (n : ‚Ñï‚àû)
            < ((n + 1 : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï‚àû) := by exact_mod_cast Nat.lt_succ_self n
          _ ‚â§ _ := _hdepth
      rw [hdepth_M_unfold] at hlt
      obtain ‚ü®k, ‚ü®rs, hrs_len, hrs_mem, hrs_reg‚ü©, hk_lt‚ü© := lt_sSup_iff.mp hlt
      have hrs_len_gt : n < rs.length := by
        rw [‚Üê hrs_len] at hk_lt
        exact_mod_cast hk_lt
      -- Step 3: rs is non-empty (length > n ‚â• 0); decompose rs = x :: rs_tail.
      rcases rs with _ | ‚ü®x, rs_tail‚ü©
      ¬∑ -- impossible: empty list has length 0, not > n.
        simp at hrs_len_gt
      have hxMem : x ‚àà IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := hrs_mem x List.mem_cons_self
      have htail_mem : ‚àÄ r ‚àà rs_tail, r ‚àà IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := fun r hr =>
        hrs_mem r (List.mem_cons_of_mem _ hr)
      have hcons := (RingTheory.Sequence.isRegular_cons_iff M x rs_tail).mp hrs_reg
      have hxReg : IsSMulRegular M x := hcons.1
      have hrs_tail_reg : RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular (QuotSMulTop x M) rs_tail :=
        hcons.2
      have htail_len_ge : n ‚â§ rs_tail.length := by
        have h1 : n < (x :: rs_tail).length := hrs_len_gt
        simp [List.length_cons] at h1
        omega
      -- Step 4: `x ‚àà Ann(Œ∫)` via `Ideal.annihilator_quotient`.
      have hannih : Module.annihilator R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R) =
          IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := Ideal.annihilator_quotient
      have hxAnnih : x ‚àà Module.annihilator R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R) :=
        hannih ‚ñ∏ hxMem
      -- Step 5: build MxM and show depth MxM ‚â• n via the prefix rs_tail.take n.
      let MxM : Type u := QuotSMulTop x M
      haveI : Nontrivial MxM :=
        nontrivial_quotSMulTop_of_mem_maximalIdeal M hxMem
      haveI : _root_.Module.Finite R MxM := inferInstance
      have hne_MxM : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ‚Ä¢ (‚ä§ : Submodule R MxM) ‚âÝ ‚ä§ :=
        Ne.symm (Submodule.top_ne_ideal_smul_of_le_jacobson_annihilator
          (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_le_jacobson _))
      let rs_n := rs_tail.take n
      have hrs_n_len : rs_n.length = n := by
        change (rs_tail.take n).length = n
        rw [List.length_take]
        omega
      have hrs_n_mem : ‚àÄ r ‚àà rs_n, r ‚àà IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := fun r hr =>
        htail_mem r (List.mem_of_mem_take hr)
      have hrs_n_reg : RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular MxM rs_n := by
        change RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular MxM (rs_tail.take n)
        have hsplit : rs_tail = rs_tail.take n ++ rs_tail.drop n :=
          (List.take_append_drop _ _).symm
        have hwr : RingTheory.Sequence.IsWeaklyRegular MxM rs_tail :=
          hrs_tail_reg.toIsWeaklyRegular
        rw [hsplit] at hwr
        have hwr_n : RingTheory.Sequence.IsWeaklyRegular MxM (rs_tail.take n) :=
          ((RingTheory.Sequence.isWeaklyRegular_append_iff MxM _ _).mp hwr).1
        exact (IsLocalRing.isRegular_iff_isWeaklyRegular_of_subset_maximalIdeal
          hrs_n_mem).mpr hwr_n
      have hdepth_MxM : (n : ‚Ñï‚àû) ‚â§ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) MxM := by
        rw [depth, if_neg hne_MxM]
        apply le_sSup
        refine ‚ü®rs_n, ?_, hrs_n_mem, hrs_n_reg‚ü©
        exact_mod_cast hrs_n_len
      -- Step 6: apply ih (M := MxM) at index n.
      have hMxM_vanish : ‚àÄ j < n, ‚àÄ e : Abelian.Ext.{u}
          (ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R))
          (ModuleCat.of R MxM) j, e = 0 :=
        (ih (M := MxM)).mp hdepth_MxM
      -- Step 7: LES chase. Set up SES.
      let S : ShortComplex (ModuleCat.{u} R) :=
        ModuleCat.smulShortComplex (ModuleCat.of R M) x
      have hS : S.ShortExact := hxReg.smulShortComplex_shortExact
      -- `S.f = x ‚Ä¢ ùüô_M` (definitional), hence `mk‚ÇÄ S.f = x ‚Ä¢ mk‚ÇÄ ùüô`, hence
      -- `_e.comp (mk‚ÇÄ S.f) (add_zero i) = x ‚Ä¢ _e = 0` (by helper at `x ‚àà Ann Œ∫`).
      have hSf_eq_smul : S.f = x ‚Ä¢ (ùüô (ModuleCat.of R M) : _ ‚ü∂ _) := rfl
      have hSf_kill :
          _e.comp (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk‚ÇÄ S.f) (add_zero i) = 0 := by
        have hcomp :
            _e.comp (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk‚ÇÄ S.f) (add_zero i) = x ‚Ä¢ _e := by
          rw [hSf_eq_smul]
          exact (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.smul_eq_comp_mk‚ÇÄ _e x).symm
        rw [hcomp]
        exact ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator _e hxAnnih
      -- Split on i = 0 vs i ‚â• 1.
      rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos i with hi0 | hi_pos
      ¬∑ subst hi0
        -- Use mono S.f (since hxReg) + postcomp_mk‚ÇÄ_injective_of_mono.
        haveI hmono : CategoryTheory.Mono S.f := by
          rw [ModuleCat.mono_iff_injective]
          exact hxReg
        have hinj := CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.postcomp_mk‚ÇÄ_injective_of_mono
          (ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R)) S.f
        apply hinj
        change _e.comp (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk‚ÇÄ S.f) (add_zero 0)
          = (0 : Abelian.Ext.{u} (ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R))
              (ModuleCat.of R M) 0).comp (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk‚ÇÄ S.f)
              (add_zero 0)
        rw [CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.zero_comp]
        exact hSf_kill
      ¬∑ -- i ‚â• 1: write i = j + 1, then j < n and use covariant_sequence_exact‚ÇÅ.
        obtain ‚ü®j, rfl‚ü© := Nat.exists_eq_succ_of_ne_zero
          (Nat.pos_iff_ne_zero.mp hi_pos)
        have hj : j < n := by omega
        obtain ‚ü®x‚ÇÉ, hx‚ÇÉ‚ü© :=
          CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact‚ÇÅ
            (ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R)) hS _e hSf_kill rfl
        have hx‚ÇÉ_zero : x‚ÇÉ = 0 := hMxM_vanish j hj _
        rw [hx‚ÇÉ_zero] at hx‚ÇÉ
        simpa using hx‚ÇÉ.symm
    ¬∑ -- (‚áê) Backward direction: `(‚àÄ i ‚â§ n, Ext^i(Œ∫, M) = 0) ‚Üí (n+1 : ‚Ñï‚àû) ‚â§ depth M`.
      intro hext
      -- Step 1: From Ext^0(Œ∫, M) = 0, extract `Subsingleton (Œ∫ ‚Üí‚Çó[R] M)`.
      -- For all R-linear maps `f g : Œ∫ ‚Üí‚Çó[R] M`, `mk‚ÇÄ (ofHom f) = 0` in Ext^0
      -- (by `hext 0`), so via `mk‚ÇÄ_eq_zero_iff` the morphism `ofHom f = 0`,
      -- hence `f = 0`.  Both `f = 0 = g`.
      have hext0 : ‚àÄ e : Abelian.Ext.{u}
          (ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R))
          (ModuleCat.of R M) 0, e = 0 := hext 0 (Nat.succ_pos n)
      have hsubsing : Subsingleton (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R ‚Üí‚Çó[R] M) := by
        refine ‚ü®fun f g => ?_‚ü©
        have hf : (ModuleCat.ofHom f : ModuleCat.of R _ ‚ü∂ ModuleCat.of R M) = 0 :=
          (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk‚ÇÄ_eq_zero_iff (ModuleCat.ofHom f)).mp
            (hext0 _)
        have hg : (ModuleCat.ofHom g : ModuleCat.of R _ ‚ü∂ ModuleCat.of R M) = 0 :=
          (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk‚ÇÄ_eq_zero_iff (ModuleCat.ofHom g)).mp
            (hext0 _)
        have hf' : f = 0 := ModuleCat.hom_ext_iff.mp hf
        have hg' : g = 0 := ModuleCat.hom_ext_iff.mp hg
        rw [hf', hg']
      -- Step 2: Apply `subsingleton_linearMap_iff` to extract a regular element.
      -- The annihilator of `R ‚ß∏ maximalIdeal R = ResidueField R` is `maximalIdeal R`
      -- by `Ideal.annihilator_quotient` (under the two-sided instance ‚Äî automatic
      -- for commutative R).
      have hannih : Module.annihilator R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R) =
          IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R :=
        Ideal.annihilator_quotient
      have ‚ü®x, hxAnnih, hxReg‚ü© :=
        IsSMulRegular.subsingleton_linearMap_iff.mp hsubsing
      have hxMem : x ‚àà IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := hannih ‚ñ∏ hxAnnih
      -- Step 3: Pass to `M/xM := QuotSMulTop x M` and apply IH at index n.
      -- iter-184+: the remaining LES chase to derive Ext-vanishing on M/xM,
      -- application of IH, and `isRegular_cons_iff` assembly closes the rest.
      -- We package the partial state in named witnesses for the next iter.
      let MxM : Type u := QuotSMulTop x M
      haveI : Nontrivial MxM :=
        nontrivial_quotSMulTop_of_mem_maximalIdeal M hxMem
      -- `Module.Finite R (M / xM)` is automatic via `Module.Finite.quotient`.
      haveI : _root_.Module.Finite R MxM := inferInstance
      -- iter-184 Lane G: closed via LES chase + sSup extraction + cons.
      --
      -- Step A: derive `‚àÄ j < n, Ext^j(Œ∫, MxM) = 0` from `hext` via the
      --   LES of `Ext^*(Œ∫, ¬∑)` on the SES `0 ‚Üí M ‚Üí[x] M ‚Üí MxM ‚Üí 0`.
      let S : ShortComplex (ModuleCat.{u} R) :=
        ModuleCat.smulShortComplex (ModuleCat.of R M) x
      have hS : S.ShortExact := hxReg.smulShortComplex_shortExact
      set Œ∫ : ModuleCat.{u} R := ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R)
        with hŒ∫
      have hMxM_vanish : ‚àÄ j < n, ‚àÄ e : Abelian.Ext.{u} Œ∫
          (ModuleCat.of R MxM) j, e = 0 := by
        intro j hj e
        -- `e.comp hS.extClass rfl : Ext Œ∫ M (j+1) = 0` by `hext` at `j+1`.
        have he_ext : e.comp hS.extClass (rfl : j + 1 = j + 1) = 0 :=
          hext (j + 1) (by omega) _
        obtain ‚ü®x‚ÇÇ, hx‚ÇÇ‚ü© :=
          CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact‚ÇÉ Œ∫ hS e rfl he_ext
        -- `x‚ÇÇ : Ext Œ∫ M j = 0` by `hext` at `j`.
        have hx‚ÇÇ_zero : x‚ÇÇ = 0 := hext j (by omega) _
        rw [hx‚ÇÇ_zero] at hx‚ÇÇ
        simpa using hx‚ÇÇ.symm
      -- Step B: apply `ih (M := MxM)` at index `n`.
      have hdepth_MxM : (n : ‚Ñï‚àû) ‚â§ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) MxM :=
        (ih (M := MxM)).mpr hMxM_vanish
      -- Step C: unfold `depth MxM = sSup`-clause and extract a witness rs'
      --   of length ‚â• n on MxM in ùî™ (when n ‚â• 1; the n = 0 case uses []).
      have hne_MxM : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ‚Ä¢ (‚ä§ : Submodule R MxM) ‚âÝ ‚ä§ :=
        Ne.symm (Submodule.top_ne_ideal_smul_of_le_jacobson_annihilator
          (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_le_jacobson _))
      have hdepth_MxM_unfold :
          depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) MxM
            = sSup { k : ‚Ñï‚àû | ‚àÉ rs : List R, (rs.length : ‚Ñï‚àû) = k ‚àß
              (‚àÄ r ‚àà rs, r ‚àà IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
              ‚àß RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular MxM rs } := by
        rw [depth, if_neg hne_MxM]
      obtain ‚ü®rs', hrs'_len_ge, hrs'_mem, hrs'_reg‚ü© :
          ‚àÉ rs' : List R, n ‚â§ rs'.length ‚àß
            (‚àÄ r ‚àà rs', r ‚àà IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ‚àß
            RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular MxM rs' := by
        rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos n with hn0 | hn_pos
        ¬∑ subst hn0
          exact ‚ü®[], by simp, by simp,
            RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular.nil R MxM‚ü©
        ¬∑ have hlt : ((n - 1 : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï‚àû) <
              depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) MxM := by
            have h1 : ((n - 1 : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï‚àû) < (n : ‚Ñï‚àû) := by
              exact_mod_cast Nat.sub_lt hn_pos Nat.one_pos
            exact h1.trans_le hdepth_MxM
          rw [hdepth_MxM_unfold] at hlt
          obtain ‚ü®k, ‚ü®rs', hrs'_len_eq, hrs'_mem, hrs'_reg‚ü©, hk_lt‚ü© :=
            lt_sSup_iff.mp hlt
          have hrs'_len_gt : n - 1 < rs'.length := by
            rw [‚Üê hrs'_len_eq] at hk_lt
            exact_mod_cast hk_lt
          refine ‚ü®rs', ?_, hrs'_mem, hrs'_reg‚ü©
          omega
      -- Step D: truncate rs' to length n; resulting sequence is M-regular.
      let rs_n := rs'.take n
      have hrs_n_len : rs_n.length = n := by
        change (rs'.take n).length = n
        rw [List.length_take]
        omega
      have hrs_n_mem : ‚àÄ r ‚àà rs_n, r ‚àà IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := fun r hr =>
        hrs'_mem r (List.mem_of_mem_take hr)
      have hrs_n_reg : RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular MxM rs_n := by
        change RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular MxM (rs'.take n)
        have hsplit : rs' = rs'.take n ++ rs'.drop n :=
          (List.take_append_drop _ _).symm
        have hwr : RingTheory.Sequence.IsWeaklyRegular MxM rs' :=
          hrs'_reg.toIsWeaklyRegular
        rw [hsplit] at hwr
        have hwr_n : RingTheory.Sequence.IsWeaklyRegular MxM (rs'.take n) :=
          ((RingTheory.Sequence.isWeaklyRegular_append_iff MxM _ _).mp hwr).1
        exact (IsLocalRing.isRegular_iff_isWeaklyRegular_of_subset_maximalIdeal
          hrs_n_mem).mpr hwr_n
      -- Step E: cons x to form a length-(n+1) M-regular sequence in ùî™.
      have hrs_M_reg : RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular M (x :: rs_n) :=
        (RingTheory.Sequence.isRegular_cons_iff M x rs_n).mpr ‚ü®hxReg, hrs_n_reg‚ü©
      have hrs_M_mem : ‚àÄ r ‚àà (x :: rs_n), r ‚àà IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := by
        intro r hr
        rcases List.mem_cons.mp hr with rfl | hr
        ¬∑ exact hxMem
        ¬∑ exact hrs_n_mem r hr
      have hrs_M_len : (x :: rs_n).length = n + 1 := by simp [hrs_n_len]
      -- Step F: conclude `(n+1 : ‚Ñï‚àû) ‚â§ depth M` via `le_sSup`.
      have hne_M : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ‚Ä¢ (‚ä§ : Submodule R M) ‚âÝ ‚ä§ :=
        Ne.symm (Submodule.top_ne_ideal_smul_of_le_jacobson_annihilator
          (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_le_jacobson _))
      rw [depth, if_neg hne_M]
      apply le_sSup
      refine ‚ü®x :: rs_n, ?_, hrs_M_mem, hrs_M_reg‚ü©
      exact_mod_cast hrs_M_len

/-! ## ¬ß4. Depth on a short exact sequence

For a short exact sequence `0 ‚Üí N' ‚Üí N ‚Üí N'' ‚Üí 0` of nonzero finite modules
over a Noetherian local ring, the three modules' depths satisfy three
crosswise inequalities (Stacks tag 00LE), each a direct read-off of the
long exact `Ext^*(Œ∫, -)` sequence and the depth-via-Ext characterisation
of ¬ß3.

Blueprint reference: `lem:depth_short_exact_sequence` (Stacks tag 00LE). -/

/-! ### Helper A (iter-182 Lane G, axiom-clean): Ext-vanishing from strict depth bound

For a Noetherian local ring `(R, ùî™)` and a nonzero finite `R`-module `M`,
if `(i : ‚Ñï‚àû) < depth M` then every element of `Ext^i_R(Œ∫, M)` is zero.

This packages `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` for the LES chase: the
`n ‚â§ depth M` form with `n := i + 1` instantiates the `‚àÄ j < i + 1`
quantifier at `j = i`. Body is kernel-clean modulo the typed sorry of
`depth_eq_smallest_ext_index`. -/
private lemma ext_vanish_of_natCast_lt_depth
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M]
    [Nontrivial M] {i : ‚Ñï}
    (h : (i : ‚Ñï‚àû) < depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M)
    (e : CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.{u}
        (ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R))
        (ModuleCat.of R M) i) : e = 0 := by
  have h' : ((i + 1 : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï‚àû) ‚â§ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M := by
    have hcast : ((i + 1 : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï‚àû) = (i : ‚Ñï‚àû) + 1 := by push_cast; ring
    rw [hcast]; exact Order.add_one_le_of_lt h
  exact (depth_eq_smallest_ext_index (M := M) (i + 1)).mp h' i (Nat.lt_succ_self i) e

/-! ### Helper B (iter-182 Lane G, axiom-clean): `‚Ñï‚àû` tsub bridge

If `(a : ‚Ñï) ‚â§ d - 1` in `‚Ñï‚àû` and `1 ‚â§ a` (in `‚Ñï`), then
`((a + 1 : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï‚àû) ‚â§ d`.

Case-split on `d = ‚ä§` (trivial) and `d = ‚Üën` (drop to `‚Ñï` arithmetic).
Used for the `depth N' - 1` shift in the second SES inequality. -/
private lemma natCast_add_one_le_of_le_sub_one
    {d : ‚Ñï‚àû} {a : ‚Ñï} (ha : 1 ‚â§ a) (h : (a : ‚Ñï‚àû) ‚â§ d - 1) :
    ((a + 1 : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï‚àû) ‚â§ d := by
  rcases eq_or_ne d ‚ä§ with hd | hd
  ¬∑ simp [hd]
  ¬∑ obtain ‚ü®n, rfl‚ü© := WithTop.ne_top_iff_exists.mp hd
    -- Reduce to ‚Ñï: turn `‚Üëa ‚â§ ‚Üën - 1` into `a ‚â§ n - 1`, then `a + 1 ‚â§ n`.
    have h‚ÇÇ : (a : ‚Ñï‚àû) ‚â§ ((n - 1 : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï‚àû) := by
      refine h.trans (le_of_eq ?_)
      rcases n with _ | n
      ¬∑ rfl
      ¬∑ push_cast; rfl
    have han : a ‚â§ n - 1 := by exact_mod_cast h‚ÇÇ
    have hle : a + 1 ‚â§ n := by omega
    exact Nat.cast_le.mpr hle

theorem depth_of_short_exact
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
    {N' N N'' : Type u}
    [AddCommGroup N'] [Module R N'] [_root_.Module.Finite R N'] [Nontrivial N']
    [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N] [_root_.Module.Finite R N] [Nontrivial N]
    [AddCommGroup N''] [Module R N''] [_root_.Module.Finite R N''] [Nontrivial N'']
    (f : N' ‚Üí‚Çó[R] N) (g : N ‚Üí‚Çó[R] N'')
    (_hf : Function.Injective f) (_hg : Function.Surjective g)
    (_hex : Function.Exact f g) :
    min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N')
        (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N'')
      ‚â§ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N
    ‚àß min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N)
          (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' - 1)
        ‚â§ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N''
    ‚àß min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N)
          (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N'' + 1)
        ‚â§ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' := by
  -- Package the SES as a `ShortComplex.ShortExact` in `ModuleCat.{u} R`.
  let S : ShortComplex (ModuleCat.{u} R) :=
    ShortComplex.mk (ModuleCat.ofHom f) (ModuleCat.ofHom g)
      (by ext x; simpa using _hex.apply_apply_eq_zero x)
  have hS : S.ShortExact :=
    ModuleCat.shortComplex_shortExact S _hex _hf _hg
  -- The residue field as a ModuleCat object.
  set Œ∫ : ModuleCat.{u} R := ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R) with hŒ∫
  refine ‚ü®?_, ?_, ?_‚ü©
  ¬∑ -- (1) min(depth N', depth N'') ‚â§ depth N
    rw [‚Üê ENat.forall_natCast_le_iff_le]
    intro a ha
    rw [le_min_iff] at ha
    obtain ‚ü®haN', haN''‚ü© := ha
    rw [depth_eq_smallest_ext_index]
    intro i hi e
    -- `e : Ext Œ∫ S.X‚ÇÇ i = Ext Œ∫ (of R N) i`; goal `e = 0`.
    have hicast : (i : ‚Ñï‚àû) < (a : ‚Ñï‚àû) := by exact_mod_cast hi
    have hiN' : (i : ‚Ñï‚àû) < depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' := hicast.trans_le haN'
    have hiN'' : (i : ‚Ñï‚àû) < depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N'' := hicast.trans_le haN''
    -- `e ‚àò S.g ‚àà Ext Œ∫ (of R N'') i = 0`.
    have heg : e.comp (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk‚ÇÄ S.g) (add_zero i) = 0 :=
      ext_vanish_of_natCast_lt_depth hiN'' _
    obtain ‚ü®x‚ÇÅ, hx‚ÇÅ‚ü© :=
      CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact‚ÇÇ Œ∫ hS e heg
    -- `x‚ÇÅ ‚àà Ext Œ∫ (of R N') i = 0`.
    have hx‚ÇÅ_zero : x‚ÇÅ = 0 := ext_vanish_of_natCast_lt_depth hiN' _
    rw [hx‚ÇÅ_zero] at hx‚ÇÅ
    simpa using hx‚ÇÅ.symm
  ¬∑ -- (2) min(depth N, depth N' - 1) ‚â§ depth N''
    rw [‚Üê ENat.forall_natCast_le_iff_le]
    intro a ha
    rw [le_min_iff] at ha
    obtain ‚ü®haN, haN'sub‚ü© := ha
    rw [depth_eq_smallest_ext_index]
    intro i hi e
    -- `e : Ext Œ∫ S.X‚ÇÉ i = Ext Œ∫ (of R N'') i`; goal `e = 0`.
    have hicast : (i : ‚Ñï‚àû) < (a : ‚Ñï‚àû) := by exact_mod_cast hi
    have hiN : (i : ‚Ñï‚àû) < depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N := hicast.trans_le haN
    -- `‚Üë(i+1) < depth N'`: use Helper B with `a` and the inequality `hi : i + 1 ‚â§ a`.
    have hia : 1 ‚â§ a := by omega
    have ha1 : ((a + 1 : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï‚àû) ‚â§ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' :=
      natCast_add_one_le_of_le_sub_one hia haN'sub
    have hsucc : ((i + 1 : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï‚àû) < depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' := by
      have : ((i + 1 : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï‚àû) < ((a + 1 : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï‚àû) := by exact_mod_cast Nat.add_lt_add_right hi 1
      exact this.trans_le ha1
    -- `e ‚àò extClass ‚àà Ext Œ∫ (of R N') (i + 1) = 0`.
    have hext : e.comp hS.extClass rfl = 0 :=
      ext_vanish_of_natCast_lt_depth hsucc _
    obtain ‚ü®x‚ÇÇ, hx‚ÇÇ‚ü© :=
      CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact‚ÇÉ Œ∫ hS e rfl hext
    -- `x‚ÇÇ ‚àà Ext Œ∫ (of R N) i = 0`.
    have hx‚ÇÇ_zero : x‚ÇÇ = 0 := ext_vanish_of_natCast_lt_depth hiN _
    rw [hx‚ÇÇ_zero] at hx‚ÇÇ
    simpa using hx‚ÇÇ.symm
  ¬∑ -- (3) min(depth N, depth N'' + 1) ‚â§ depth N'
    rw [‚Üê ENat.forall_natCast_le_iff_le]
    intro a ha
    rw [le_min_iff] at ha
    obtain ‚ü®haN, haN''add‚ü© := ha
    rw [depth_eq_smallest_ext_index]
    intro i hi e
    -- `e : Ext Œ∫ S.X‚ÇÅ i = Ext Œ∫ (of R N') i`; goal `e = 0`.
    have hicast : (i : ‚Ñï‚àû) < (a : ‚Ñï‚àû) := by exact_mod_cast hi
    have hiN : (i : ‚Ñï‚àû) < depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N := hicast.trans_le haN
    -- `e ‚àò S.f ‚àà Ext Œ∫ (of R N) i = 0`.
    have hef : e.comp (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk‚ÇÄ S.f) (add_zero i) = 0 :=
      ext_vanish_of_natCast_lt_depth hiN _
    -- Split on `i = 0` vs `i ‚â• 1`. For `i ‚â• 1`, use `covariant_sequence_exact‚ÇÅ`.
    -- For `i = 0`, postcomposition by `S.f` is injective (since `S.f` is mono).
    rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos i with hi0 | hi0
    ¬∑ subst hi0
      -- `e : Ext Œ∫ S.X‚ÇÅ 0`; postcomp by `S.f` is injective; image is `e ‚àò S.f = 0`,
      -- so `e = 0`.
      have hmono : CategoryTheory.Mono S.f :=
        (ModuleCat.mono_iff_injective _).mpr _hf
      have hinj := CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.postcomp_mk‚ÇÄ_injective_of_mono Œ∫ S.f
      apply hinj
      simpa using hef
    ¬∑ -- `i ‚â• 1`. Let `i = j + 1` and use `covariant_sequence_exact‚ÇÅ` at
      -- `n‚ÇÄ = j, n‚ÇÅ = i = j + 1`.
      obtain ‚ü®j, rfl‚ü© := Nat.exists_eq_succ_of_ne_zero (Nat.pos_iff_ne_zero.mp hi0)
      -- `e : Ext Œ∫ (of R N') (j + 1)`. We need `Ext Œ∫ (of R N'') j = 0`.
      -- From `‚Üë(j+2) ‚â§ ‚Üëa ‚â§ depth N'' + 1`, get `‚Üëj + 1 ‚â§ depth N''`, so `‚Üëj < depth N''`.
      have hjN'' : (j : ‚Ñï‚àû) < depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N'' := by
        have hja : j + 2 ‚â§ a := by omega
        have h_j2 : ((j + 2 : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï‚àû) ‚â§ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N'' + 1 := by
          refine le_trans ?_ haN''add
          exact_mod_cast hja
        have hcast : ((j + 2 : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï‚àû) = ((j + 1 : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï‚àû) + 1 := by push_cast; ring
        rw [hcast] at h_j2
        have h_canc : ((j + 1 : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï‚àû) ‚â§ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N'' :=
          (ENat.add_le_add_iff_right (by norm_num : (1 : ‚Ñï‚àû) ‚âÝ ‚ä§)).mp h_j2
        have hcast2 : ((j + 1 : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï‚àû) = (j : ‚Ñï‚àû) + 1 := by push_cast; ring
        rw [hcast2] at h_canc
        exact (ENat.add_one_le_iff (by simp : (j : ‚Ñï‚àû) ‚âÝ ‚ä§)).mp h_canc
      obtain ‚ü®x‚ÇÉ, hx‚ÇÉ‚ü© :=
        CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact‚ÇÅ Œ∫ hS e hef rfl
      -- `x‚ÇÉ ‚àà Ext Œ∫ (of R N'') j = 0`.
      have hx‚ÇÉ_zero : x‚ÇÉ = 0 := ext_vanish_of_natCast_lt_depth hjN'' _
      rw [hx‚ÇÉ_zero] at hx‚ÇÉ
      simpa using hx‚ÇÉ.symm

/-! ### Helper iter-193 Lane G (axiom-clean): depth is preserved under R-linear equivalence

For a commutative ring `R`, an ideal `I ‚äÜ R`, and two `R`-modules `M, M'` with
an `R`-linear equivalence `e : M ‚âÉ‚Çó[R] M'`, we have `depth I M = depth I M'`.

This is the standard "depth is an invariant of the isomorphism class" fact;
the proof has two steps: (1) the side-condition `I ‚Ä¢ ‚ä§ = ‚ä§` is preserved
under linear equivalence, and (2) the regular-sequence supremum sets agree
via `LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr`. Both halves are direct Mathlib calls; the
helper is axiom-clean.

iter-193 Lane G structural advance: this helper is the substrate piece
needed to identify `depth(M)` with `depth(R^k)` for `M` finite free, which
in turn closes the `pd(M) = 0` base case of the Auslander‚ÄìBuchsbaum
formula modulo the `depth(R^k) = depth(R)` direct-sum step (a separate
substrate item; the file's residual sorries are narrowed to that one
named piece). -/
lemma depth_eq_of_linearEquiv {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (I : Ideal R)
    {M M' : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [AddCommGroup M'] [Module R M']
    (e : M ‚âÉ‚Çó[R] M') :
    depth I M = depth I M' := by
  -- Step 1: `I ‚Ä¢ ‚ä§ = ‚ä§` is preserved under the linear equivalence.
  have hcond : (I ‚Ä¢ (‚ä§ : Submodule R M) = ‚ä§) ‚Üî (I ‚Ä¢ (‚ä§ : Submodule R M') = ‚ä§) := by
    have e_top : Submodule.map (e : M ‚Üí‚Çó[R] M') (‚ä§ : Submodule R M) = ‚ä§ := by
      rw [Submodule.map_top]; exact LinearEquiv.range e
    have e_symm_top :
        Submodule.map (e.symm : M' ‚Üí‚Çó[R] M) (‚ä§ : Submodule R M') = ‚ä§ := by
      rw [Submodule.map_top]; exact LinearEquiv.range e.symm
    refine ‚ü®?_, ?_‚ü©
    ¬∑ intro h
      have hmap :=
        Submodule.map_smul'' I (‚ä§ : Submodule R M) (e : M ‚Üí‚Çó[R] M')
      rw [h, e_top] at hmap
      exact hmap.symm
    ¬∑ intro h
      have hmap :=
        Submodule.map_smul'' I (‚ä§ : Submodule R M') (e.symm : M' ‚Üí‚Çó[R] M)
      rw [h, e_symm_top] at hmap
      exact hmap.symm
  -- Step 2: the `sSup` sets agree via `LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr`.
  unfold depth
  by_cases h : I ‚Ä¢ (‚ä§ : Submodule R M) = ‚ä§
  ¬∑ simp [if_pos h, if_pos (hcond.mp h)]
  ¬∑ rw [if_neg h, if_neg (mt hcond.mpr h)]
    congr 1
    ext n
    refine ‚ü®?_, ?_‚ü©
    ¬∑ rintro ‚ü®rs, hlen, hmem, hreg‚ü©
      exact ‚ü®rs, hlen, hmem, (LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr e rs).mp hreg‚ü©
    ¬∑ rintro ‚ü®rs, hlen, hmem, hreg‚ü©
      exact ‚ü®rs, hlen, hmem, (LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr e rs).mpr hreg‚ü©

/-! ### Helper iter-194 Lane G (axiom-clean): depth of constant Pi equals depth of fiber.

For a commutative ring `R`, ideal `I`, module `M`, and nonempty finite type `Œπ`,
`depth I (Œπ ‚Üí M) = depth I M`. The proof goes through the regular-sequence
characterization: each `r`-action on `Œπ ‚Üí M` is pointwise (so an `r ‚àà R` is
regular on `Œπ ‚Üí M` iff regular on `M`), and the quotient `(Œπ ‚Üí M)/r¬∑‚ä§`
identifies with `Œπ ‚Üí M/r¬∑‚ä§` via `Submodule.quotientPi`. The side condition
`I ‚Ä¢ ‚ä§ = ‚ä§` agrees on both sides via a `Pi.single` lifting argument.

This is the substrate needed to close the `pd_R(M) = 0` case of the
Auslander‚ÄìBuchsbaum formula (where `M ‚âÉ‚Çó[R] Fin k ‚Üí R` via a basis). -/

/-- For any commutative ring `R`, ideal `I`, finite index `Œπ`, and module `M`,
the ideal-action `I ‚Ä¢ ‚ä§_{Œπ ‚Üí M}` equals the pi-submodule of fibre `I ‚Ä¢ ‚ä§_M`s. -/
private lemma ideal_smul_top_pi_const
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {Œπ : Type*} [Fintype Œπ] [DecidableEq Œπ]
    (I : Ideal R) {M : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] :
    (I ‚Ä¢ (‚ä§ : Submodule R (Œπ ‚Üí M))) =
      Submodule.pi (Set.univ : Set Œπ) (fun (_ : Œπ) => I ‚Ä¢ (‚ä§ : Submodule R M)) := by
  apply le_antisymm
  ¬∑ intro f hf i _
    refine Submodule.smul_induction_on hf ?_ ?_
    ¬∑ intro a hain x _
      change a ‚Ä¢ x i ‚àà I ‚Ä¢ (‚ä§ : Submodule R M)
      exact Submodule.smul_mem_smul hain trivial
    ¬∑ intro x y hx hy
      change (x + y) i ‚àà _
      exact Submodule.add_mem _ hx hy
  ¬∑ intro f hf
    rw [show f = ‚àë j, Pi.single j (f j) from (Finset.univ_sum_single f).symm]
    refine Submodule.sum_mem _ ?_
    intro j _
    have hfj : f j ‚àà I ‚Ä¢ (‚ä§ : Submodule R M) := hf j (Set.mem_univ j)
    have hmap :
        Pi.single j (f j) ‚àà
          Submodule.map (LinearMap.single R (fun (_ : Œπ) => M) j)
            (I ‚Ä¢ (‚ä§ : Submodule R M)) :=
      Submodule.mem_map.mpr ‚ü®f j, hfj, rfl‚ü©
    rw [Submodule.map_smul''] at hmap
    exact Submodule.smul_mono le_rfl le_top hmap

/-- The side condition `I ‚Ä¢ ‚ä§ = ‚ä§` agrees on `Œπ ‚Üí M` and `M` for nonempty
finite `Œπ`: a free product of fibre `I‚Ä¢‚ä§_M`-witnesses combines to a
`I‚Ä¢‚ä§_{Œπ ‚Üí M}`-witness (via `Pi.single`-lifting), and conversely a
`Pi.single j m`-projection at `j` reads off the witness on the fibre. -/
private lemma ideal_smul_top_pi_const_eq_top_iff
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {Œπ : Type*} [Fintype Œπ] [DecidableEq Œπ] [Nonempty Œπ]
    (I : Ideal R) {M : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] :
    I ‚Ä¢ (‚ä§ : Submodule R (Œπ ‚Üí M)) = ‚ä§ ‚Üî I ‚Ä¢ (‚ä§ : Submodule R M) = ‚ä§ := by
  constructor
  ¬∑ intro h
    rw [eq_top_iff]
    intro m _
    obtain ‚ü®j‚ü© := ‚ÄπNonempty Œπ‚Ä∫
    have hsingle_mem :
        (Pi.single j m : Œπ ‚Üí M) ‚àà I ‚Ä¢ (‚ä§ : Submodule R (Œπ ‚Üí M)) := by
      rw [h]; trivial
    rw [ideal_smul_top_pi_const] at hsingle_mem
    have := hsingle_mem j (Set.mem_univ j)
    rwa [Pi.single_eq_same] at this
  ¬∑ intro h
    rw [ideal_smul_top_pi_const, eq_top_iff]
    intro f _ i _
    rw [h]
    trivial

/-- `QuotSMulTop r (Œπ ‚Üí M) ‚âÉ‚Çó[R] Œπ ‚Üí QuotSMulTop r M` for finite `Œπ`,
obtained by rewriting `r ‚Ä¢ ‚ä§ = Ideal.span {r} ‚Ä¢ ‚ä§` and using
`Submodule.quotientPi`. -/
private noncomputable def quotSMulTopPiConstLinearEquiv
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {Œπ : Type*} [Fintype Œπ] [DecidableEq Œπ] (r : R)
    {M : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] :
    QuotSMulTop r (Œπ ‚Üí M) ‚âÉ‚Çó[R] (Œπ ‚Üí QuotSMulTop r M) := by
  refine (Submodule.quotEquivOfEq _ _ ?_).trans (Submodule.quotientPi _)
  rw [‚Üê Submodule.ideal_span_singleton_smul r (‚ä§ : Submodule R (Œπ ‚Üí M))]
  rw [ideal_smul_top_pi_const]
  congr 1
  funext _
  exact Submodule.ideal_span_singleton_smul r ‚ä§

/-- For nonempty finite `Œπ`, a list `rs : List R` is `(Œπ ‚Üí M)`-regular iff it
is `M`-regular. Proof by induction on `rs`: the empty case reduces to
`Nontrivial (Œπ ‚Üí M) ‚Üî Nontrivial M`; the cons case uses `Pi.isSMulRegular_iff`
(for the SMul-regular conjunct) plus `quotSMulTopPiConstLinearEquiv` +
`LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr` (to bridge the quotient regularity to the IH on
`QuotSMulTop r M`). -/
private lemma isRegular_pi_const_iff_of_nonempty
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {Œπ : Type*} [Fintype Œπ] [DecidableEq Œπ] [Nonempty Œπ]
    (rs : List R) :
    ‚àÄ {M : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M],
      RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular (Œπ ‚Üí M) rs ‚Üî
        RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular M rs := by
  induction rs with
  | nil =>
    intro M _ _
    refine ‚ü®?_, ?_‚ü©
    ¬∑ rintro ‚ü®_, hPi_top‚ü©
      refine ‚ü®.nil R M, ?_‚ü©
      rw [Ideal.ofList_nil, Submodule.bot_smul] at hPi_top ‚ä¢
      intro habs
      apply hPi_top
      rw [Submodule.eq_bot_iff] at habs ‚ä¢
      intro f _
      funext i
      exact habs (f i) trivial
    ¬∑ rintro ‚ü®_, hM_top‚ü©
      refine ‚ü®.nil R (Œπ ‚Üí M), ?_‚ü©
      rw [Ideal.ofList_nil, Submodule.bot_smul] at hM_top ‚ä¢
      obtain ‚ü®j‚ü© := ‚ÄπNonempty Œπ‚Ä∫
      intro habs
      apply hM_top
      rw [Submodule.eq_bot_iff] at habs ‚ä¢
      intro m _
      have hsingle : (Pi.single j m : Œπ ‚Üí M) = 0 := habs _ trivial
      have heval := congr_fun hsingle j
      rwa [Pi.single_eq_same, Pi.zero_apply] at heval
  | cons r rs' ih =>
    intro M _ _
    rw [RingTheory.Sequence.isRegular_cons_iff, RingTheory.Sequence.isRegular_cons_iff]
    refine and_congr ?_ ?_
    ¬∑ constructor
      ¬∑ intro h
        obtain ‚ü®j‚ü© := ‚ÄπNonempty Œπ‚Ä∫
        exact Pi.isSMulRegular_iff.mp h j
      ¬∑ intro h
        exact Pi.isSMulRegular_iff.mpr fun _ => h
    ¬∑ rw [LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr
        (quotSMulTopPiConstLinearEquiv (R := R) (Œπ := Œπ) r (M := M)) rs']
      exact ih (M := QuotSMulTop r M)

/-- **Main iter-194 Lane G closure.** For any commutative ring `R`, ideal `I`,
`R`-module `M`, and nonempty finite type `Œπ`, the depth of the Pi module
`Œπ ‚Üí M` equals the depth of `M`:
```
  depth I (Œπ ‚Üí M) = depth I M.
```
This is the substrate for the `pd_R(M) = 0` case of the Auslander‚ÄìBuchsbaum
formula: a finite free module `M ‚âÉ‚Çó[R] Fin k ‚Üí R` has `depth(M) = depth(R)`,
so `0 + depth(M) = depth(R)` holds. -/
lemma depth_pi_const_eq_depth_of_nonempty
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (I : Ideal R)
    {Œπ : Type*} [Fintype Œπ] [DecidableEq Œπ] [Nonempty Œπ]
    {M : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] :
    depth I (Œπ ‚Üí M) = depth I M := by
  unfold depth
  by_cases h : I ‚Ä¢ (‚ä§ : Submodule R (Œπ ‚Üí M)) = ‚ä§
  ¬∑ rw [if_pos h, if_pos ((ideal_smul_top_pi_const_eq_top_iff I).mp h)]
  ¬∑ rw [if_neg h, if_neg (mt (ideal_smul_top_pi_const_eq_top_iff I).mpr h)]
    congr 1
    ext n
    refine ‚ü®?_, ?_‚ü©
    ¬∑ rintro ‚ü®rs, hlen, hmem, hreg‚ü©
      exact ‚ü®rs, hlen, hmem, (isRegular_pi_const_iff_of_nonempty rs).mp hreg‚ü©
    ¬∑ rintro ‚ü®rs, hlen, hmem, hreg‚ü©
      exact ‚ü®rs, hlen, hmem, (isRegular_pi_const_iff_of_nonempty rs).mpr hreg‚ü©

/-! ### Helper iter-198 Lane AB (axiom-clean): depth drops by exactly one for a regular
element (Stacks `lemma-depth-drops-by-one`).

For a Noetherian local ring `(R, ùî™)`, a nonzero finite `R`-module `M`, and `x ‚àà ùî™` an
`M`-regular element, the depth of `M/xM` is exactly one less than the depth of `M`:
`depth(M/xM) + 1 = depth(M)`.

This is one of the four substrate gaps for the Auslander‚ÄìBuchsbaum n=k+1 step (the
"depth-drops-by-one" piece). The proof packages the long-exact sequence of
`Ext^*(Œ∫, -)` on the SES `0 ‚Üí M ‚Üí[x] M ‚Üí M/xM ‚Üí 0`, using that
`[x]` is zero on every `Ext^*(Œ∫, M)` (since `x ‚àà Ann Œ∫`) to break the LES into short
exact pieces `0 ‚Üí Ext^i(Œ∫, M) ‚Üí Ext^i(Œ∫, M/xM) ‚Üí Ext^{i+1}(Œ∫, M) ‚Üí 0`.

two-direction bridge that converts a natural-number indexed predicate to a `‚Ñï‚àû` inequality. -/
lemma depth_quotSMulTop_succ_eq_depth_of_isSMulRegular
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M]
    [Nontrivial M] {x : R} (hxMem : x ‚àà IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
    (hxReg : IsSMulRegular M x) :
    depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (QuotSMulTop x M) + 1 =
      depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M := by
  haveI hMxM_nt : Nontrivial (QuotSMulTop x M) :=
    nontrivial_quotSMulTop_of_mem_maximalIdeal M hxMem
  haveI : _root_.Module.Finite R (QuotSMulTop x M) := inferInstance
  -- Œ∫ as a ModuleCat for compact notation.
  set Œ∫ : ModuleCat.{u} R := ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R) with hŒ∫
  -- x annihilates Œ∫.
  have hxAnnih : x ‚àà _root_.Module.annihilator R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R) := by
    have h : _root_.Module.annihilator R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R)
        = IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := Ideal.annihilator_quotient
    rw [h]; exact hxMem
  -- The SES `0 ‚Üí M ‚Üí[x] M ‚Üí M/xM ‚Üí 0`.
  let S : ShortComplex (ModuleCat.{u} R) :=
    ModuleCat.smulShortComplex (ModuleCat.of R M) x
  have hS : S.ShortExact := hxReg.smulShortComplex_shortExact
  have hSf_eq_smul : S.f = x ‚Ä¢ (ùüô (ModuleCat.of R M) : _ ‚ü∂ _) := rfl
  -- `[x]_*` is zero on every `Ext^i(Œ∫, M)` via the helper `ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator`.
  have hSf_kill : ‚àÄ {i : ‚Ñï} (e : Abelian.Ext.{u} Œ∫ (ModuleCat.of R M) i),
      e.comp (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk‚ÇÄ S.f) (add_zero i) = 0 := by
    intro i e
    have hcomp :
        e.comp (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk‚ÇÄ S.f) (add_zero i) = x ‚Ä¢ e := by
      rw [hSf_eq_smul]
      exact (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.smul_eq_comp_mk‚ÇÄ e x).symm
    rw [hcomp]
    exact ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator e hxAnnih
  refine le_antisymm ?_ ?_
  ¬∑ -- (‚â•): depth(M/xM) + 1 ‚â§ depth M.
    rw [‚Üê ENat.forall_natCast_le_iff_le]
    intro a ha
    rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos a with h0 | hpos
    ¬∑ subst h0; exact bot_le
    ¬∑ obtain ‚ü®n, rfl‚ü© := Nat.exists_eq_succ_of_ne_zero (Nat.pos_iff_ne_zero.mp hpos)
      have hcast : ((n + 1 : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï‚àû) = (n : ‚Ñï‚àû) + 1 := by push_cast; ring
      have hcast' : (Nat.succ n : ‚Ñï) = n + 1 := rfl
      rw [hcast'] at ha
      rw [hcast] at ha
      have hone_ne_top : (1 : ‚Ñï‚àû) ‚âÝ ‚ä§ := by simp
      have hn_le_MxM :
          (n : ‚Ñï‚àû) ‚â§ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (QuotSMulTop x M) :=
        (ENat.add_le_add_iff_right hone_ne_top).mp ha
      have hMxM_vanish : ‚àÄ j : ‚Ñï, j < n ‚Üí ‚àÄ e : Abelian.Ext.{u} Œ∫
            (ModuleCat.of R (QuotSMulTop x M)) j, e = 0 :=
        (depth_eq_smallest_ext_index (M := QuotSMulTop x M) n).mp hn_le_MxM
      change ((n.succ : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï‚àû) ‚â§ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M
      rw [hcast']
      rw [depth_eq_smallest_ext_index]
      intro i hi e
      rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos i with hi0 | hi_pos
      ¬∑ subst hi0
        -- i = 0 case: x being M-regular ‚üπ S.f mono ‚üπ postcomp_mk‚ÇÄ_injective_of_mono.
        haveI hmono : CategoryTheory.Mono S.f := by
          rw [ModuleCat.mono_iff_injective]
          exact hxReg
        have hinj := CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.postcomp_mk‚ÇÄ_injective_of_mono Œ∫ S.f
        apply hinj
        change e.comp (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk‚ÇÄ S.f) (add_zero 0)
          = (0 : Abelian.Ext.{u} Œ∫ (ModuleCat.of R M) 0).comp
              (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk‚ÇÄ S.f) (add_zero 0)
        rw [CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.zero_comp]
        exact hSf_kill e
      ¬∑ -- i ‚â• 1: write i = j + 1, use `covariant_sequence_exact‚ÇÅ`.
        obtain ‚ü®j, rfl‚ü© := Nat.exists_eq_succ_of_ne_zero
          (Nat.pos_iff_ne_zero.mp hi_pos)
        have hj : j < n := by omega
        obtain ‚ü®x‚ÇÉ, hx‚ÇÉ‚ü© :=
          CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact‚ÇÅ Œ∫ hS e (hSf_kill e) rfl
        have hx‚ÇÉ_zero : x‚ÇÉ = 0 := hMxM_vanish j hj _
        rw [hx‚ÇÉ_zero] at hx‚ÇÉ
        simpa using hx‚ÇÉ.symm
  ¬∑ -- (‚â§): depth M ‚â§ depth(M/xM) + 1.
    rw [‚Üê ENat.forall_natCast_le_iff_le]
    intro a ha
    rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos a with h0 | hpos
    ¬∑ subst h0; exact bot_le
    ¬∑ obtain ‚ü®m, rfl‚ü© := Nat.exists_eq_succ_of_ne_zero (Nat.pos_iff_ne_zero.mp hpos)
      have hcast' : (Nat.succ m : ‚Ñï) = m + 1 := rfl
      rw [hcast'] at ha
      have hM_vanish : ‚àÄ i : ‚Ñï, i < m + 1 ‚Üí ‚àÄ e : Abelian.Ext.{u} Œ∫
            (ModuleCat.of R M) i, e = 0 :=
        (depth_eq_smallest_ext_index (M := M) (m + 1)).mp ha
      have hcast : ((m + 1 : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï‚àû) = (m : ‚Ñï‚àû) + 1 := by push_cast; ring
      change ((m.succ : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï‚àû) ‚â§ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (QuotSMulTop x M) + 1
      rw [hcast', hcast]
      -- It suffices to show m ‚â§ depth(M/xM).
      have hMxM_bound :
          (m : ‚Ñï‚àû) ‚â§ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (QuotSMulTop x M) := by
        rw [depth_eq_smallest_ext_index]
        intro j hj e
        -- Use `covariant_sequence_exact‚ÇÉ` at n‚ÇÄ = j, n‚ÇÅ = j + 1.
        have he_extClass : e.comp hS.extClass (rfl : j + 1 = j + 1) = 0 :=
          hM_vanish (j + 1) (by omega) _
        obtain ‚ü®x‚ÇÇ, hx‚ÇÇ‚ü© :=
          CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact‚ÇÉ Œ∫ hS e rfl he_extClass
        have hx‚ÇÇ_zero : x‚ÇÇ = 0 := hM_vanish j (by omega) _
        rw [hx‚ÇÇ_zero] at hx‚ÇÇ
        simpa using hx‚ÇÇ.symm
      gcongr

/-! ### Helper iter-198 Lane AB (axiom-clean): regular-element existence from positive depth.

For a Noetherian local ring `(R, ùî™)` and a nonzero finite `R`-module `M`, the assertion
`1 ‚â§ depth_ùî™(M)` is equivalent to the existence of an `M`-regular element `x ‚àà ùî™`. This
is a direct consequence of unfolding the depth definition to a regular-sequence supremum
(Nakayama rules out the `ùî™ ‚Ä¢ ‚ä§ = ‚ä§` trivialisation in the `Nontrivial M` setting) and
reading off a length-1 witness.

This is the substrate companion to `depth_quotSMulTop_succ_eq_depth_of_isSMulRegular`: the
two together implement the Stacks tag 00LE step "depth-drops-by-one for any regular
element" plus its prerequisite "the regular element exists". -/
lemma exists_isSMulRegular_of_one_le_depth
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M]
    [Nontrivial M]
    (h : (1 : ‚Ñï‚àû) ‚â§ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M) :
    ‚àÉ x : R, x ‚àà IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R ‚àß IsSMulRegular M x := by
  -- Unfold depth: `ùî™ ‚Ä¢ ‚ä§ ‚âÝ ‚ä§` by Nakayama in the `Nontrivial M` finite setting.
  have hne_M : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ‚Ä¢ (‚ä§ : Submodule R M) ‚âÝ ‚ä§ :=
    Ne.symm (Submodule.top_ne_ideal_smul_of_le_jacobson_annihilator
      (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_le_jacobson _))
  have hdepth_unfold :
      depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M
        = sSup { k : ‚Ñï‚àû | ‚àÉ rs : List R, (rs.length : ‚Ñï‚àû) = k ‚àß
          (‚àÄ r ‚àà rs, r ‚àà IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
          ‚àß RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular M rs } := by
    rw [depth, if_neg hne_M]
  rw [hdepth_unfold] at h
  have hlt : (0 : ‚Ñï‚àû) < sSup { k : ‚Ñï‚àû | ‚àÉ rs : List R, (rs.length : ‚Ñï‚àû) = k ‚àß
      (‚àÄ r ‚àà rs, r ‚àà IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
      ‚àß RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular M rs } := by
    calc (0 : ‚Ñï‚àû) < 1 := by norm_num
      _ ‚â§ _ := h
  obtain ‚ü®k, ‚ü®rs, hrs_len, hrs_mem, hrs_reg‚ü©, hk_lt‚ü© := lt_sSup_iff.mp hlt
  -- rs has positive length (since (0 : ‚Ñï‚àû) < ‚Üërs.length).
  have hrs_pos : 0 < rs.length := by
    by_contra h_neg
    push Not at h_neg
    have hk0 : rs.length = 0 := Nat.le_zero.mp h_neg
    rw [hk0] at hrs_len
    simp only [Nat.cast_zero] at hrs_len
    rw [‚Üê hrs_len] at hk_lt
    exact absurd hk_lt (lt_irrefl _)
  rcases rs with _ | ‚ü®x, rs_tail‚ü©
  ¬∑ simp at hrs_pos
  ¬∑ refine ‚ü®x, hrs_mem x List.mem_cons_self, ?_‚ü©
    exact ((RingTheory.Sequence.isRegular_cons_iff M x rs_tail).mp hrs_reg).1

/-! ### Helper iter-199 Lane AB-gap1 (axiom-clean): minimal surjection substrate.

For a finite `R`-module `M` over a local ring `R`, there exists a surjective
`R`-linear map `f : (Fin n ‚Üí R) ‚Üí‚Çó[R] M` of the **minimal possible rank**
`n = dim_Œ∫ (Œ∫ ‚äó_R M)` (where `Œ∫ = R/ùî™` is the residue field) whose **kernel
is contained in `ùî™ ‚Ä¢ ‚ä§`**. This is the first step of constructing a *minimal
finite free resolution*: iterating the construction on the kernel (which is
itself finitely generated when `R` is Noetherian) produces successive
syzygies whose differential maps each have image in `ùî™` times their target.

This is the first substrate piece of gap (1) (Stacks
`lemma-add-trivial-complex`) in the Auslander‚ÄìBuchsbaum closure chain
(`auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd`). It packages the basic
**Nakayama-lift** of a Œ∫-basis of `Œ∫ ‚äó_R M` to an `R`-spanning family in `M`
and reads off the kernel-containment from linear independence of the basis
combined with the `1 ‚äó_R -` evaluation.

Mathlib substrate used (all axiom-clean):
* `IsLocalRing.span_eq_top_of_tmul_eq_basis` ‚Äî Nakayama lift of a Œ∫-basis.
* `TensorProduct.mk_surjective` ‚Äî the `1 ‚äó_R -` map is surjective for the
  residue-field tensor.
* `Module.Basis.constr_range` ‚Äî range of the linear extension equals span of
  the chosen image set.
* `Module.Basis.linearIndependent` ‚Äî independence of a Œ∫-basis.
* `IsLocalRing.residue_eq_zero_iff` ‚Äî `r ‚àà ùî™ ‚Üî residue r = 0`. -/
lemma exists_minimalSurjection_finite_localRing
    (R : Type u) [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
    (M : Type u) [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M] :
    ‚àÉ (n : ‚Ñï) (f : (Fin n ‚Üí R) ‚Üí‚Çó[R] M),
      Function.Surjective f ‚àß
      n = _root_.Module.finrank (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R)
        (TensorProduct R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R) M) ‚àß
      LinearMap.ker f ‚â§ (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ‚Ä¢ ‚ä§ := by
  set Œ∫ := IsLocalRing.ResidueField R with hŒ∫
  set n := _root_.Module.finrank Œ∫ (TensorProduct R Œ∫ M) with hn
  -- Pick a Œ∫-basis of `Œ∫ ‚äó_R M`.
  let b : _root_.Module.Basis (Fin n) Œ∫ (TensorProduct R Œ∫ M) :=
    _root_.Module.finBasis Œ∫ (TensorProduct R Œ∫ M)
  -- The canonical map `(1 : Œ∫) ‚äó_R -` is surjective.
  have hsurj_mk : Function.Surjective ((TensorProduct.mk R Œ∫ M) 1) := by
    apply TensorProduct.mk_surjective
    exact Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
  -- Lift each basis element to a representative in M.
  choose lift hlift using hsurj_mk
  let m : Fin n ‚Üí M := fun i => lift (b i)
  have hm : ‚àÄ i, (1 : Œ∫) ‚äó‚Çú[R] m i = b i := fun i => hlift (b i)
  -- Define `f` by sending each standard basis vector of `Fin n ‚Üí R` to `m i`.
  let f : (Fin n ‚Üí R) ‚Üí‚Çó[R] M := (Pi.basisFun R (Fin n)).constr R m
  -- Evaluation: `f x = Œ£ x i ‚Ä¢ m i`.
  have hf_eval : ‚àÄ x : Fin n ‚Üí R, f x = ‚àë i, x i ‚Ä¢ m i := by
    intro x
    rw [show f x = ((Pi.basisFun R (Fin n)).constr R) m x from rfl,
        _root_.Module.Basis.constr_apply]
    have h : (Pi.basisFun R (Fin n)).repr x = Finsupp.equivFunOnFinite.symm x := by
      ext i; rw [Pi.basisFun_repr]; rfl
    rw [h, Finsupp.sum_fintype _ _ (by intros; simp)]
    exact Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => by simp)
  -- Range = span of `m`.
  have hf_range : LinearMap.range f = Submodule.span R (Set.range m) :=
    _root_.Module.Basis.constr_range _ _
  -- Nakayama: span of `m i` equals all of `M`.
  have hspan : Submodule.span R (Set.range m) = ‚ä§ :=
    IsLocalRing.span_eq_top_of_tmul_eq_basis m b hm
  refine ‚ü®n, f, ?_, rfl, ?_‚ü©
  ¬∑ exact LinearMap.range_eq_top.mp (by rw [hf_range, hspan])
  ¬∑ -- Kernel containment in `ùî™ ‚Ä¢ ‚ä§`.
    intro x hx
    have hfx : f x = 0 := hx
    rw [hf_eval] at hfx
    -- Apply `(1 : Œ∫) ‚äó_R -` to `Œ£ x i ‚Ä¢ m i = 0`.
    have h1 : (1 : Œ∫) ‚äó‚Çú[R] (‚àë i, x i ‚Ä¢ m i) = (0 : TensorProduct R Œ∫ M) := by
      rw [hfx]; exact TensorProduct.tmul_zero _ _
    rw [TensorProduct.tmul_sum] at h1
    -- Rewrite each summand: `1 ‚äó_R (x i ‚Ä¢ m i) = residue(x i) ‚Ä¢ b i`.
    have hrewrite : ‚àÄ i, (1 : Œ∫) ‚äó‚Çú[R] (x i ‚Ä¢ m i)
        = (IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) : Œ∫) ‚Ä¢ b i := by
      intro i
      rw [show ((1 : Œ∫) ‚äó‚Çú[R] (x i ‚Ä¢ m i))
          = x i ‚Ä¢ ((1 : Œ∫) ‚äó‚Çú[R] m i) from
        (TensorProduct.tmul_smul (R := R) (x i) (1 : Œ∫) (m i))]
      rw [hm i]; rfl
    rw [show (‚àë i, (1 : Œ∫) ‚äó‚Çú[R] (x i ‚Ä¢ m i))
        = ‚àë i, (IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) : Œ∫) ‚Ä¢ b i from
      Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => hrewrite i)] at h1
    -- Linear independence of `b` forces each `residue (x i) = 0`.
    have hlin : LinearIndependent Œ∫ b := b.linearIndependent
    have hall : ‚àÄ i, (IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) : Œ∫) = 0 := by
      have := Fintype.linearIndependent_iff.mp hlin
        (fun i => IsLocalRing.residue R (x i)) h1
      exact fun i => this i
    -- Convert each component-in-ùî™ to `x ‚àà ùî™ ‚Ä¢ ‚ä§` via `Pi.single` decomposition.
    have hx_pi : ‚àÄ i, x i ‚àà IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := by
      intro i
      have : IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) = 0 := hall i
      rwa [IsLocalRing.residue_eq_zero_iff] at this
    rw [show x = ‚àë i, Pi.single i (x i) from (Finset.univ_sum_single x).symm]
    refine Submodule.sum_mem _ ?_
    intro i _
    have hsingle :
        (Pi.single i (x i) : Fin n ‚Üí R)
          = (x i) ‚Ä¢ (Pi.single i (1 : R) : Fin n ‚Üí R) := by
      ext j; by_cases hij : i = j <;> simp [Pi.single, Function.update, hij]
    rw [hsingle]
    exact Submodule.smul_mem_smul (hx_pi i) trivial

/-! ### Helper iter-200 Lane AB-gap1-HasPdLT (axiom-clean): bridge from
`projectiveDimension` equation to `HasProjectiveDimensionLT`.

Converts the `Module.projectiveDimension R M = ((n : ‚Ñï) : WithBot ‚Ñï‚àû)` hypothesis

lemma hasProjectiveDimensionLT_succ_of_projectiveDimension_eq
    {R : Type u} [Ring R] {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] {n : ‚Ñï}

/-! ### Helper iter-200 Lane AB-gap1-HasPdLT (axiom-clean): syzygy descent via
`hasProjectiveDimensionLT_X‚ÇÅ`.

For a surjection `f : R^n ‚Üí‚Çó M` and a bound `HasProjectiveDimensionLT M (k+2)`

lemma hasProjectiveDimensionLT_ker_of_surjection

    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]
    {n : ‚Ñï} (f : (Fin n ‚Üí R) ‚Üí‚Çó[R] M) (hf : Function.Surjective f)

/-! ### Helper iter-200 Lane AB-gap1-HasPdLT (axiom-clean): projective-dimension
