/-! ### Helper iter-200 Lane AB-gap1-HasPdLT (axiom-clean): depth lower bound on
the kernel of a surjection from a finite free module.

For a surjection `f : R^n ‚Üí‚Çó M` from a finite free module of rank `n ‚â• 1` over a
Noetherian local ring `(R, ùî™)`, with both `M` and `ker f` nontrivial, the depth
of `ker f` admits the lower bound
```
  min(depth_ùî™ R, depth_ùî™ M + 1) ‚â§ depth_ùî™ (ker f).
```
This is Stacks 00LE (3) (`depth_of_short_exact` part 3) applied to the
SES `0 ‚Üí ker f ‚Üí R^n ‚Üí M ‚Üí 0`, combined with `depth_pi_const_eq_depth_of_nonempty`
to identify `depth(R^n)` with `depth(R)`.

This is the ‚â• direction of the LES analysis on the SES; the matching ‚â§ direction
(`depth(ker f) ‚â§ min(depth R, depth M + 1)`) is the residual gap needed for the
inductive step of the Auslander‚ÄìBuchsbaum formula. It requires either the "what
is exact" criterion (Stacks 00MF) or a refined LES argument when
`depth M < depth R`; the current substrate provides only the easy direction. -/
lemma depth_ker_ge_min_of_surjection_finite_localRing
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M] [Nontrivial M]
    {n : ‚Ñï} (hn : 1 ‚â§ n) (f : (Fin n ‚Üí R) ‚Üí‚Çó[R] M) (hf : Function.Surjective f)
    [Nontrivial (LinearMap.ker f)] :
    min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R)
        (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M + 1)
      ‚â§ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (LinearMap.ker f) := by
  -- Set up instances on the auxiliary modules.
  haveI : Inhabited (Fin n) := ‚ü®‚ü®0, hn‚ü©‚ü©
  haveI : Nonempty (Fin n) := ‚ü®default‚ü©
  haveI : Nontrivial (Fin n ‚Üí R) := Pi.nontrivial
  haveI : _root_.Module.Finite R (LinearMap.ker f) := Module.IsNoetherian.finite R _
  -- The SES: 0 ‚Üí ker f ‚Üí R^n ‚Üí M ‚Üí 0.
  have hex : Function.Exact (LinearMap.ker f).subtype f :=
    LinearMap.exact_subtype_ker_map f
  have hinj : Function.Injective (LinearMap.ker f).subtype :=
    Subtype.val_injective
  -- Apply `depth_of_short_exact` part (3).
  have htriple := depth_of_short_exact (LinearMap.ker f).subtype f hinj hf hex
  have h3 := htriple.2.2
  -- Rewrite `depth (Fin n ‚Üí R) = depth R`.
  have heq : depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (Fin n ‚Üí R)
      = depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R :=
    depth_pi_const_eq_depth_of_nonempty _
  rw [heq] at h3
  exact h3

/-! ### Helper iter-202 Lane AB Path B (axiom-clean): both `depth_of_short_exact`
inequalities (parts (2) and (3)) for the SES `0 ‚Üí ker f ‚Üí R^n ‚Üí M ‚Üí 0`.

Packages parts (2) and (3) of Stacks 00LE applied to the kernel SES of a
surjection `f : R^n ‚ÜÝ M` from a finite free module of rank `n ‚â• 1` over a
Noetherian local ring, after identifying `depth(R^n) = depth(R)` via
`depth_pi_const_eq_depth_of_nonempty`. These are precisely the two inequalities
fed to `enat_ab_inductive_combine` to close the inductive step of the
Auslander‚ÄìBuchsbaum formula. -/
lemma depth_ses_ineqs_of_surjection_finite_localRing
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M] [Nontrivial M]
    {n : ‚Ñï} (hn : 1 ‚â§ n) (f : (Fin n ‚Üí R) ‚Üí‚Çó[R] M) (hf : Function.Surjective f)
    [Nontrivial (LinearMap.ker f)] :
    min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R)
        (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (LinearMap.ker f) - 1)
      ‚â§ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M
    ‚àß min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R)
          (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M + 1)
      ‚â§ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (LinearMap.ker f) := by
  haveI : Inhabited (Fin n) := ‚ü®‚ü®0, hn‚ü©‚ü©
  haveI : Nonempty (Fin n) := ‚ü®default‚ü©
  haveI : Nontrivial (Fin n ‚Üí R) := Pi.nontrivial
  haveI : _root_.Module.Finite R (LinearMap.ker f) := Module.IsNoetherian.finite R _
  have hex : Function.Exact (LinearMap.ker f).subtype f :=
    LinearMap.exact_subtype_ker_map f
  have hinj : Function.Injective (LinearMap.ker f).subtype :=
    Subtype.val_injective
  have htriple := depth_of_short_exact (LinearMap.ker f).subtype f hinj hf hex
  have heq : depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (Fin n ‚Üí R)
      = depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R :=
    depth_pi_const_eq_depth_of_nonempty _
  refine ‚ü®?_, ?_‚ü©
  ¬∑ have h2 := htriple.2.1
    rwa [heq] at h2
  ¬∑ have h3 := htriple.2.2
    rwa [heq] at h3

/-! ### Helper iter-202 Lane AB Path B (axiom-clean): nonzero `Ext` at the depth
index.

The converse read-off of `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index`: for a nonzero finite
module `M` of depth exactly `‚ÜëD` over a Noetherian local ring, there is a
nonzero element of `Ext^D_R(Œ∫, M)`. (Below `‚ÜëD` all `Ext` vanish; the strict
failure of vanishing at `‚Üë(D+1)` must therefore occur at index `D`.) This is
the input that exhibits a nonzero class in the base case of Auslander‚ÄìBuchsbaum:
`Ext^{depth R}(Œ∫, R^k) ‚âÝ 0`. -/
lemma exists_ne_zero_ext_of_depth_eq
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M]
    [Nontrivial M] {D : ‚Ñï}
    (hD : depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M = (D : ‚Ñï‚àû)) :
    ‚àÉ e : Abelian.Ext.{u} (ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R))
        (ModuleCat.of R M) D, e ‚âÝ 0 := by
  -- Below `D`, all Ext vanish.
  have hvanish : ‚àÄ i : ‚Ñï, i < D ‚Üí ‚àÄ e : Abelian.Ext.{u}
      (ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R)) (ModuleCat.of R M) i, e = 0 :=
    (depth_eq_smallest_ext_index (M := M) D).mp (by rw [hD])
  -- `‚Üë(D+1) ‚â§ depth M` fails, so vanishing below `D+1` fails.
  have hnle : ¬¨ ((D + 1 : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï‚àû) ‚â§ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M := by
    rw [hD, Nat.cast_le]; omega
  rw [depth_eq_smallest_ext_index (M := M) (D + 1)] at hnle
  push_neg at hnle
  obtain ‚ü®i, hi, e, he‚ü© := hnle
  have hiD : i = D := by
    by_contra hne
    have : i < D := by omega
    exact he (hvanish i this e)
  subst hiD
  exact ‚ü®e, he‚ü©

/-! ### Helper iter-201 Lane AB Path B (axiom-clean): matrix decomposition and
matrix-collapse on `Ext`.

For an R-linear map `A : R^m ‚Üí‚Çó R^n` between standard free modules over a
commutative ring R, A decomposes as `A = ‚àë_{(i,j)} A_{i,j} ‚Ä¢ E_{i,j}` where
`A_{i,j} = (A (Pi.single j 1)) i` is the matrix entry and `E_{i,j}` is the
"elementary" linear map sending `Pi.single j 1 ‚Ü¶ Pi.single i 1`. From this
decomposition combined with R-bilinearity of `Ext.comp` and the axiom-clean
helper `ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator`, we obtain the matrix-collapse
result: if every entry of A lies in `Ann_R N`, then the induced postcomposition
`Ext^p(N, R^m) ‚Üí Ext^p(N, R^n)` via `mk‚ÇÄ (ofHom A)` is the zero map.

This is the key new substrate for closing the **Path B base case `pd M = 1`**
of the Auslander‚ÄìBuchsbaum formula: given a minimal surjection `f : R^n ‚ÜÝ M`
with `ker f` free of positive rank, the inclusion `ker f ‚âÖ R^k ‚Ü™ R^n` is an
R-linear map with entries in ùî™ (by minimality `ker f ‚â§ ùî™ ‚Ä¢ ‚ä§`); the
matrix-collapse then forces the LES injectivity needed to conclude
`depth M < depth R`. -/

/-- The "elementary matrix" linear map `E_{i,j} : R^m ‚Üí‚Çó R^n` sending
`Pi.single j 1 ‚Ü¶ Pi.single i 1` and all other standard basis vectors to 0. -/
private def elemMap {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (n m : ‚Ñï) (i : Fin n) (j : Fin m) :
    (Fin m ‚Üí R) ‚Üí‚Çó[R] (Fin n ‚Üí R) :=
  (LinearMap.toSpanSingleton R (Fin n ‚Üí R) (Pi.single i (1 : R) : Fin n ‚Üí R)) ‚àò‚Çó
    (LinearMap.proj (R := R) (œÜ := fun _ : Fin m => R) j)

/-- The elementary map `E_{i,j}` evaluated at `x : R^m` gives `Pi.single i (x j)`. -/
private lemma elemMap_apply {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (n m : ‚Ñï)
    (i : Fin n) (j : Fin m) (x : Fin m ‚Üí R) :
    (elemMap n m i j : (Fin m ‚Üí R) ‚Üí‚Çó[R] (Fin n ‚Üí R)) x = Pi.single i (x j) := by
  classical
  change (LinearMap.toSpanSingleton R (Fin n ‚Üí R)
    (Pi.single i (1 : R) : Fin n ‚Üí R)) (x j) = Pi.single i (x j)
  rw [LinearMap.toSpanSingleton_apply]
  ext k
  rw [Pi.smul_apply]
  by_cases hk : k = i
  ¬∑ subst hk; rw [Pi.single_eq_same, Pi.single_eq_same, smul_eq_mul, mul_one]
  ¬∑ rw [Pi.single_eq_of_ne hk, Pi.single_eq_of_ne hk, smul_zero]

/-- Matrix decomposition: every R-linear map `A : R^m ‚Üí‚Çó R^n` can be written
as a sum `‚àë_{(i,j)} A (Pi.single j 1) i ‚Ä¢ elemMap n m i j` of elementary maps
weighted by matrix entries. -/
private lemma linearMap_finFunR_matrix_decomp {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {n m : ‚Ñï}
    (A : (Fin m ‚Üí R) ‚Üí‚Çó[R] (Fin n ‚Üí R)) :
    A = ‚àë ij : Fin n √ó Fin m, (A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1) ‚Ä¢ elemMap n m ij.1 ij.2 := by
  classical
  refine LinearMap.ext fun (x : Fin m ‚Üí R) => ?_
  rw [LinearMap.sum_apply]
  rw [show A x = ‚àë j : Fin m, ‚àë i : Fin n,
      (A (Pi.single j 1) i) ‚Ä¢ (Pi.single i (x j) : Fin n ‚Üí R) from ?_]
  ¬∑ rw [‚Üê Finset.univ_product_univ, Finset.sum_product_right]
    refine Finset.sum_congr rfl fun j _ => ?_
    refine Finset.sum_congr rfl fun i _ => ?_
    rw [LinearMap.smul_apply, elemMap_apply]
  ¬∑ have hx_decomp : x = ‚àë j : Fin m, (x j) ‚Ä¢ (Pi.single j (1 : R) : Fin m ‚Üí R) := by
      ext k
      rw [Finset.sum_apply, Finset.sum_eq_single k]
      ¬∑ simp
      ¬∑ intro b _ hb
        rw [Pi.smul_apply, Pi.single_eq_of_ne hb.symm, smul_zero]
      ¬∑ intro h; exact absurd (Finset.mem_univ k) h
    conv_lhs => rw [hx_decomp, map_sum]
    refine Finset.sum_congr rfl fun j _ => ?_
    rw [map_smul]
    ext k
    rw [Pi.smul_apply, Finset.sum_apply]
    rw [Finset.sum_eq_single k]
    ¬∑ rw [Pi.smul_apply, Pi.single_eq_same, smul_eq_mul, smul_eq_mul, mul_comm]
    ¬∑ intro b _ hb
      rw [Pi.smul_apply, Pi.single_eq_of_ne (Ne.symm hb), smul_zero]
    ¬∑ intro h; exact absurd (Finset.mem_univ k) h

/-- **Matrix-collapse on Ext.** For an R-linear map `A : R^m ‚Üí‚Çó R^n` whose every
matrix entry `A (Pi.single j 1) i` lies in `Ann_R N`, the postcomposition map
`Ext^p(N, R^m) ‚Üí Ext^p(N, R^n)` induced by `mk‚ÇÄ (ofHom A)` is the zero map.

Proof: write `A = ‚àë_{(i,j)} A_{i,j} ‚Ä¢ E_{i,j}` via `linearMap_finFunR_matrix_decomp`.
Push through `ofHom`, `mk‚ÇÄ`, and `Ext.comp` using `ofHom_sum / mk‚ÇÄ_sum / comp_sum`
plus `ofHom_smul / mk‚ÇÄ_smul / comp_smul`. Each summand becomes
`A_{i,j} ‚Ä¢ (e.comp (mk‚ÇÄ (ofHom (elemMap _ _ i j))))`, where the scalar `A_{i,j}`
lies in `Ann_R N`. The existing `ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator` (Stacks
00LP fragment) makes each such scalar action zero. Hence the total sum is zero. -/
private lemma ext_comp_mk‚ÇÄ_ofHom_eq_zero_of_entries_mem_annihilator
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R]
    {N : ModuleCat.{u} R}
    {n m : ‚Ñï}
    (A : (Fin m ‚Üí R) ‚Üí‚Çó[R] (Fin n ‚Üí R))
    (hA : ‚àÄ (i : Fin n) (j : Fin m),
        A (Pi.single j 1) i ‚àà _root_.Module.annihilator R (N : Type u))
    {p : ‚Ñï} (e : Abelian.Ext.{u} N (ModuleCat.of R (Fin m ‚Üí R)) p) :
    e.comp (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk‚ÇÄ
              (ModuleCat.ofHom A :
                ModuleCat.of R (Fin m ‚Üí R) ‚ü∂ ModuleCat.of R (Fin n ‚Üí R)))
          (add_zero p) = 0 := by
  classical
  rw [linearMap_finFunR_matrix_decomp A]
  rw [show (ModuleCat.ofHom (‚àë ij : Fin n √ó Fin m,
      A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1 ‚Ä¢ elemMap n m ij.1 ij.2) :
      ModuleCat.of R (Fin m ‚Üí R) ‚ü∂ ModuleCat.of R (Fin n ‚Üí R))
      = ‚àë ij : Fin n √ó Fin m, ModuleCat.ofHom
          (A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1 ‚Ä¢ elemMap n m ij.1 ij.2) from by
    refine ModuleCat.hom_ext ?_
    rw [ModuleCat.hom_sum]
    rfl]
  rw [CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk‚ÇÄ_sum]
  rw [CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.comp_sum]
  apply Finset.sum_eq_zero
  intro ij _
  rw [show (ModuleCat.ofHom (A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1 ‚Ä¢ elemMap n m ij.1 ij.2) :
      ModuleCat.of R (Fin m ‚Üí R) ‚ü∂ ModuleCat.of R (Fin n ‚Üí R))
      = A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1 ‚Ä¢ ModuleCat.ofHom (elemMap n m ij.1 ij.2) from by
    refine ModuleCat.hom_ext ?_; rfl]
  rw [show (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk‚ÇÄ
      (A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1 ‚Ä¢ ModuleCat.ofHom (elemMap n m ij.1 ij.2)) :
        Abelian.Ext.{u} (ModuleCat.of R (Fin m ‚Üí R)) (ModuleCat.of R (Fin n ‚Üí R)) 0)
      = A (Pi.single ij.2 1) ij.1 ‚Ä¢ CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk‚ÇÄ
          (ModuleCat.ofHom (elemMap n m ij.1 ij.2)) from
      CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk‚ÇÄ_smul (R := R) _ _]
  rw [CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.comp_smul]
  exact ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator _ (hA ij.1 ij.2)

end Module

/-! ## ¬ß5. The Auslander‚ÄìBuchsbaum formula

For a nonzero finite module `M` of finite projective dimension over a
Noetherian local ring `(R, ùî™)`, the **Auslander‚ÄìBuchsbaum formula** reads
```
  pd_R(M) + depth(M) = depth(R)
```
(Stacks tag 090V). The proof inducts on `depth(M)`: the base case
`depth(M) = 0` uses a minimal finite free resolution of `M` and the
"what is exact" criterion (Stacks 00MF) plus iterated application of the
depth-on-a-short-exact-sequence lemma (¬ß4) to bound `depth(R)` against the
resolution length; the inductive step picks a common non-zero-divisor
`x ‚àà ùî™` on both `R` and `M`, applies the snake lemma to obtain a minimal
finite free resolution of `M/xM` over `R/xR` of the same length, and uses
the inductive hypothesis on `M/xM` over `R/xR`.

Blueprint reference: `thm:auslander_buchsbaum` (Stacks tag 090V). -/

/-! ### Helper iter-202 Lane AB Path B (axiom-clean): ‚Ñï‚àû combine for the
inductive step of Auslander‚ÄìBuchsbaum.

Pure arithmetic in `‚Ñï‚àû = WithTop ‚Ñï`: packages the inductive hypothesis
`j + depth(K) = depth(R)` together with the two `depth_of_short_exact`
inequalities (parts (2) and (3)) on the SES `0 ‚Üí K ‚Üí R^n ‚Üí M ‚Üí 0` ‚Äî after
identifying `depth(R^n) = depth(R)` ‚Äî into the conclusion
`(j+1) + depth(M) = depth(R)`. Valid for `j ‚â• 1` (the inductive step
`pd M ‚â• 2`); the `j = 0` / `pd M = 1` base case is handled separately via
matrix-collapse since part (3) is then vacuous. -/
private lemma enat_ab_inductive_combine {j : ‚Ñï} {d dK dM : ‚Ñï‚àû}
    (hIH : (j : ‚Ñï‚àû) + dK = d)
    (h2 : min d (dK - 1) ‚â§ dM)
    (h3 : min d (dM + 1) ‚â§ dK)
    (hj : 1 ‚â§ j) :
    ((j + 1 : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï‚àû) + dM = d := by
  subst hIH
  cases dK with
  | top =>
    -- `dK = ‚ä§`: part (2) forces `dM = ‚ä§`, both sides are `‚ä§`.
    have hdM : dM = ‚ä§ := by
      have : (‚ä§ : ‚Ñï‚àû) ‚â§ dM := by simpa using h2
      simpa using top_le_iff.mp this
    subst hdM; simp
  | coe K =>
    cases dM with
    | top =>
      -- `dM = ‚ä§`: part (3) gives `‚Üë(j+K) ‚â§ ‚ÜëK`, i.e. `j ‚â§ 0`, contradiction.
      exfalso
      have : (j : ‚Ñï‚àû) + (K : ‚Ñï‚àû) ‚â§ (K : ‚Ñï‚àû) := by simpa using h3
      rw [‚Üê Nat.cast_add, Nat.cast_le] at this
      omega
    | coe m =>
      -- All finite: reduce parts (2),(3) to `‚Ñï` disjunctions and finish with `omega`.
      have h2' : min ((j : ‚Ñï‚àû) + (K : ‚Ñï‚àû)) ((K : ‚Ñï‚àû) - 1) ‚â§ (m : ‚Ñï‚àû) := h2
      have h3' : min ((j : ‚Ñï‚àû) + (K : ‚Ñï‚àû)) ((m : ‚Ñï‚àû) + 1) ‚â§ (K : ‚Ñï‚àû) := h3
      rw [show ((K : ‚Ñï‚àû) - 1) = ((K - 1 : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï‚àû) by
            cases K with
            | zero => simp
            | succ K => push_cast; rfl,
          ‚Üê Nat.cast_add] at h2'
      rw [show ((m : ‚Ñï‚àû) + 1) = ((m + 1 : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï‚àû) by push_cast; rfl,
          ‚Üê Nat.cast_add] at h3'
      simp only [min_le_iff, Nat.cast_le] at h2' h3'
      rw [‚Üê Nat.cast_add, ‚Üê Nat.cast_add, Nat.cast_inj]
      omega

/-! ### Helper iter-202 Lane AB Path B (axiom-clean): exact projective dimension
of the syzygy `ker f`.

For a surjection `f : R^n ‚ÜÝ M` with `pd_R M = k+2`, the kernel satisfies
`pd_R (ker f) = k+1` exactly. The upper bound `‚â§ k+1` is the syzygy-descent
helper `hasProjectiveDimensionLT_ker_of_surjection` packaged through
`projectiveDimension_le_iff`; the lower bound `‚â• k+1` is the contrapositive of
the ascent helper `hasProjectiveDimensionLT_succ_of_hasProjectiveDimensionLT_ker`
through `projectiveDimension_ge_iff` (if `pd(ker f) < k+1` then `pd M < k+2`,
contradiction). This is the exact-pd input the inductive step needs to invoke
its induction hypothesis on `ker f`. -/
private lemma projectiveDimension_ker_eq_of_surjection
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R]
    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]
    {n : ‚Ñï} (f : (Fin n ‚Üí R) ‚Üí‚Çó[R] M) (hf : Function.Surjective f)
    {k : ‚Ñï}
    (hpdM : _root_.Module.projectiveDimension R M = ((k + 2 : ‚Ñï) : WithBot ‚Ñï‚àû)) :
    _root_.Module.projectiveDimension R (LinearMap.ker f) = ((k + 1 : ‚Ñï) : WithBot ‚Ñï‚àû) := by
  have hM3 : HasProjectiveDimensionLT (ModuleCat.of R M) (k + 2 + 1) :=
    Module.hasProjectiveDimensionLT_succ_of_projectiveDimension_eq hpdM
  have hK2 : HasProjectiveDimensionLT (ModuleCat.of R (LinearMap.ker f)) (k + 2) :=
    Module.hasProjectiveDimensionLT_ker_of_surjection f hf (k := k + 1) hM3
  have hle : _root_.Module.projectiveDimension R (LinearMap.ker f)
      ‚â§ ((k + 1 : ‚Ñï) : WithBot ‚Ñï‚àû) := by
    rw [show _root_.Module.projectiveDimension R (LinearMap.ker f)
          = CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension (ModuleCat.of R (LinearMap.ker f)) from rfl,
        CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension_le_iff]
    exact hK2
  have hge : ((k + 1 : ‚Ñï) : WithBot ‚Ñï‚àû)
      ‚â§ _root_.Module.projectiveDimension R (LinearMap.ker f) := by
    rw [show _root_.Module.projectiveDimension R (LinearMap.ker f)
          = CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension (ModuleCat.of R (LinearMap.ker f)) from rfl,
        CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension_ge_iff]
    intro hK1
    have hM2 : HasProjectiveDimensionLT (ModuleCat.of R M) (k + 2) :=
      Module.hasProjectiveDimensionLT_succ_of_hasProjectiveDimensionLT_ker f hf (k := k) hK1
    have hlt : CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension (ModuleCat.of R M)
        < ((k + 2 : ‚Ñï) : WithBot ‚Ñï‚àû) :=
      CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension_lt_iff.mpr hM2
    rw [show CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension (ModuleCat.of R M)
          = _root_.Module.projectiveDimension R M from rfl, hpdM] at hlt
    exact absurd hlt (lt_irrefl _)
  exact le_antisymm hle hge

lemma auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd

    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M]
    [Nontrivial M] (k : ‚Ñï)

  -- inductive step `pd M = k+2` is closed via the syzygy descent (IH on

  -- inequalities, combined arithmetically by `enat_ab_inductive_combine`. The
  -- base case `pd M = 1` is the matrix-collapse argument (Path B), using

  induction k generalizing M with

    -- (pd K = 0); writing `K ‚âÖ R^k`, the inclusion `A : R^k ‚Ü™ R^n` has
    -- entries in `ùî™`. The matrix-collapse forces the LES of `Ext^*(Œ∫, -)` to
    -- give `depth M + 1 = depth R`; combined with `depth_of_short_exact (2)`.

      Module.exists_minimalSurjection_finite_localRing R M
    -- `ker f` is projective (pd < 1), finite, hence free over the local ring.

      Module.hasProjectiveDimensionLT_succ_of_projectiveDimension_eq _hpd
    haveI hK_lt : HasProjectiveDimensionLT (ModuleCat.of R (LinearMap.ker f)) 1 :=
      Module.hasProjectiveDimensionLT_ker_of_surjection f hf_surj (k := 0) hM_lt
    haveI hKproj : CategoryTheory.Projective (ModuleCat.of R (LinearMap.ker f)) :=
      inferInstance

      (IsProjective.iff_projective _).mpr hKproj
    haveI : _root_.Module.Flat R (LinearMap.ker f) := _root_.Module.Flat.of_projective
    haveI : _root_.Module.Finite R (LinearMap.ker f) := Module.IsNoetherian.finite R _

        (IsProjective.iff_projective M).mp inferInstance

        CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension_lt_iff.mpr hMlt1

    have hdRk : Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (Fin k ‚Üí R)
        = Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R :=
      Module.depth_pi_const_eq_depth_of_nonempty _
    -- Direction (A): `depth R - 1 ‚â§ depth M` from `depth_of_short_exact (2)`.
    have htriple := Module.depth_of_short_exact A f hAinj hf_surj hexact
    have hpart2 : Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R - 1
        ‚â§ Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M := by
      have h := htriple.2.1

        = IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := Ideal.annihilator_quotient

        A (Pi.single j 1) i ‚àà _root_.Module.annihilator R

      intro i j

          ‚àà (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ‚Ä¢ (‚ä§ : Submodule R (Fin n ‚Üí R)) :=
        hf_min (œÜ (Pi.single j 1)).2

      have hcoord : A (Pi.single j 1) i
          ‚àà (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ‚Ä¢ (‚ä§ : Submodule R R) := hvec i (Set.mem_univ i)

          change Œ±.comp (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk‚ÇÄ (ModuleCat.ofHom A)) (add_zero 0)
              = (0 : Abelian.Ext.{u} Œ∫ (ModuleCat.of R (Fin k ‚Üí R)) 0).comp

/-- **The Auslander‚ÄìBuchsbaum formula.** Let `(R, ùî™)` be a Noetherian local
ring and let `M` be a nonzero finite `R`-module of finite projective
dimension. Then
```
  pd_R(M) + depth_R(M) = depth(R).
```

The hypothesis "finite projective dimension" is encoded by an explicit
upper bound `n : ‚Ñï` on the projective dimension (so the formula compares
finite numeric quantities cleanly without `WithBot ‚Ñï‚àû`-arithmetic
subtleties).

iter-195 Lane G: the inductive step `pd_R(M) = k + 1` is delegated to
the structural-carving helper `auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd`,
which packages the entire substrate gap (4 named Mathlib-absent pieces)
into a single typed `sorry` with a concrete iter-196+ re-engagement
plan. The base case `pd_R(M) = 0` closed kernel-clean iter-194 via the
finite-free-module + `depth_pi_const_eq_depth_of_nonempty` route. -/
theorem auslander_buchsbaum_formula
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M]
    [Nontrivial M]
    (n : ‚Ñï)
    (_hpd : _root_.Module.projectiveDimension R M = (n : WithBot ‚Ñï‚àû)) :
    (n : ‚Ñï‚àû) + Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M
      = Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R := by
  -- iter-193 Lane G: structural scaffold for Stacks 090V. We split on `n` to
  -- isolate the base case `pd_R(M) = 0` (where `M` is finite free over a
  -- Noetherian local ring) from the inductive step `pd_R(M) = k + 1` (which
  -- inducts on `depth(M)` via the snake-lemma-on-minimal-resolution recipe).
  --
  -- The `depth_eq_of_linearEquiv` helper above (axiom-clean) is the substrate
  -- piece used in the `n = 0` case to identify `depth(M)` with `depth(R^k)`
  -- for `M ‚âÉ‚Çó[R] (Fin k ‚Üí R)`; the residual `depth(R^k) = depth(R)` step is
  -- the remaining substrate gap (a single direct-sum-of-modules depth fact).
  rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos n with hn0 | hn_pos
  ¬∑ -- **Base case `n = 0`**: `pd_R(M) = 0` ‚üπ `M` projective ‚üπ (finite + local)
    -- `M` free ‚üπ `depth(M) = depth(R)`. The formula
    -- `0 + depth(M) = depth(R)` collapses to `depth(M) = depth(R)`.
    subst hn0
    rw [Nat.cast_zero, zero_add]
    -- Step 1: unfold `_root_.Module.projectiveDimension` to the categorical
    -- form, then apply `projectiveDimension_eq_zero_iff`.
    have hpd' :
        CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension (ModuleCat.of R M) = (0 : WithBot ‚Ñï‚àû) := by
      unfold _root_.Module.projectiveDimension at _hpd
      exact _hpd
    obtain ‚ü®hproj, _hNonZero‚ü© :=
      (CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension_eq_zero_iff _).mp hpd'
    -- Step 2: `CategoryTheory.Projective (ModuleCat.of R M)` ‚üπ `Module.Projective R M`.
    have hMproj : _root_.Module.Projective R M :=
      (IsProjective.iff_projective M).mpr hproj
    -- Step 3: `Module.Projective` ‚üπ `Module.Flat`.
    haveI : _root_.Module.Flat R M := _root_.Module.Flat.of_projective
    -- Step 4: `Module.Flat` + `IsLocalRing` + `Module.Finite` ‚üπ `Module.Free`.
    haveI : _root_.Module.Free R M := _root_.Module.free_of_flat_of_isLocalRing
    -- Step 5: with `M` finite free + `Nontrivial`, identify
    -- `depth(M) = depth(R)` via the `Module.finBasis` equivalence and the
    -- axiom-clean `depth_eq_of_linearEquiv` helper. The residual is the
    -- single, narrower statement: `depth(Fin k ‚Üí R) = depth(R)` for `k ‚â• 1`.
    have hk : 0 < _root_.Module.finrank R M :=
      (_root_.Module.finrank_pos_iff_of_free R M).mpr inferInstance
    set k : ‚Ñï := _root_.Module.finrank R M with hk_def
    -- Build the equivalence `M ‚âÉ‚Çó[R] (Fin k ‚Üí R)` via the chosen basis.
    let e : M ‚âÉ‚Çó[R] (Fin k ‚Üí R) := (_root_.Module.finBasis R M).equivFun
    -- Transport `depth(M) = depth(Fin k ‚Üí R)` using the axiom-clean helper.
    have hdepth_M_eq : Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M
        = Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (Fin k ‚Üí R) :=
      Module.depth_eq_of_linearEquiv _ e
    rw [hdepth_M_eq]
    -- iter-194 Lane G HARD BAR closure: invoke the new axiom-clean helper
    -- `Module.depth_pi_const_eq_depth_of_nonempty` which proves the substrate
    -- `depth(Œπ ‚Üí M) = depth(M)` for nonempty finite `Œπ` (Pi-quotient
    -- decomposition + regular-sequence transport).
    haveI : Nonempty (Fin k) := ‚ü®‚ü®0, hk‚ü©‚ü©
    exact Module.depth_pi_const_eq_depth_of_nonempty _
  ¬∑ -- **Inductive step `n = k + 1`**: delegate to the iter-195 Lane G
    -- structural-carving helper `auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd`,
    -- whose docstring documents the 4 substrate-gap pieces and the
    -- concrete iter-196+ re-engagement plan.
    obtain ‚ü®k, rfl‚ü© :=
      Nat.exists_eq_succ_of_ne_zero (Nat.pos_iff_ne_zero.mp hn_pos)
    exact auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd k _hpd

/-! ### Narrow substrate gap (Stacks 00NQ): regular local ring is a domain

The Auslander‚ÄìBuchsbaum-route closure of "regular local ring is Cohen‚ÄìMacaulay"
ultimately reduces to **Stacks 00NQ**: every regular local Noetherian ring
is an integral domain. Mathlib at the pinned commit `b80f227` does not expose

Stacks 00NQ proof sketch (induction on `dim R = spanFinrank ùî™`):
* Base `dim R = 0`: then `ùî™ = ‚ä•`, hence `R` is a field, hence a domain.
* Step `dim R = d + 1 ‚â• 1`:
  - Pick `x ‚àà ùî™ \ ùî™¬≤` (axiom-clean via Nakayama; see
    `exists_notMemSq_of_spanFinrank_pos` below).
  - By the cotangent dim-drop (`finrank_cotangentSpace_quot_span_singleton_succ`,
    iter-188 Lane G1 closure) **plus** Krull's principal ideal theorem
    (`ringKrullDim_quotient_span_singleton_succ_eq_ringKrullDim_of_mem_jacobson`
    gives the `‚â•` half), `R / (x)` is regular local of dim `d`.
  - By induction, `R / (x)` is a domain, hence `(x)` is a prime ideal of `R`.
  - Let `ùî≠` be a minimal prime of `R` with `ùî≠ ‚äÜ (x)`. For `y ‚àà ùî≠`, write

    immediately give a contradiction but combined with `dim R/(x) = d` and
    `x ‚àà ùî≠` height-zero we get `ùî≠ = 0` which is the conclusion), we get
    `z ‚àà ùî≠`. Hence `ùî≠ ‚äÜ x ùî≠`, so iterating, `ùî≠ ‚äÜ ‚à© x^n ùî≠ ‚äÜ ‚à© ùî™^n = 0`

/-- **Axiom-clean Nakayama witness.** For a Noetherian local ring `(R, ùî™)`
with `spanFinrank ùî™ ‚â• 1`, there exists `x ‚àà ùî™` with `x ‚àâ ùî™¬≤`.

This is the "cotangent space is nonzero" content: by Nakayama, if `ùî™ ‚äÜ ùî™¬≤`
then `ùî™ = 0` (so `spanFinrank ùî™ = 0`), contradicting the hypothesis. -/
private lemma exists_notMemSq_of_spanFinrank_pos
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
    (h : 0 < (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank) :
    ‚àÉ x ‚àà IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R, x ‚àâ (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ^ 2 := by
  -- By contradiction: assume ùî™ ‚äÜ ùî™¬≤, then by Nakayama ùî™ = 0, but spanFinrank ùî™ ‚â• 1.
  by_contra h_neg
  push Not at h_neg
  have hùî™_le_sq : IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R ‚â§ (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ^ 2 := h_neg
  -- ùî™¬≤ = ùî™ ‚Ä¢ ùî™.
  have hsq : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Submodule R R) ^ 2
      = (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ‚Ä¢ (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Submodule R R) := by
    rw [sq, ‚Üê Ideal.smul_eq_mul]
  have hùî™_fg : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Submodule R R).FG :=
    Ideal.fg_of_isNoetherianRing _
  -- Use Submodule.le_of_le_smul_of_le_jacobson_bot with N = ‚ä•.
  have hjac : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ‚â§ (‚ä• : Ideal R).jacobson :=
    IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_le_jacobson _
  have h_le_smul : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Submodule R R)
      ‚â§ ‚ä• ‚äî (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ‚Ä¢
        (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Submodule R R) := by
    rw [bot_sup_eq, ‚Üê hsq]; exact hùî™_le_sq
  have hùî™_bot : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Submodule R R) ‚â§ ‚ä• :=
    Submodule.le_of_le_smul_of_le_jacobson_bot hùî™_fg hjac h_le_smul
  have hùî™_eq_bot : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) = ‚ä• := le_bot_iff.mp hùî™_bot
  have h_span : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = 0 := by
    rw [hùî™_eq_bot]; exact Submodule.spanFinrank_bot
  omega

/-! ### Helper for Stacks 00NU inductive step prep (iter-190 Lane G, axiom-clean).

For a regular local Noetherian ring `R` of `spanFinrank ùî™ = k + 1` and
`x ‚àà ùî™ \ ùî™¬≤`, the quotient `R ‚ß∏ Ideal.span {x}` is again a regular local
ring of `spanFinrank ùî™' = k`.

This is the **axiom-clean** counterpart of
`exists_isSMulRegular_quotient_isRegularLocal_succ`: it avoids the
`IsSMulRegular R x` hypothesis (which depends on `isDomain_of_regularLocal`)
by routing the dimension lower bound through

lemma regularLocal_quotient_isRegularLocal_of_notMemSq
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
    [IsRegularLocalRing R] {k : ‚Ñï}
    (hdim : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = k + 1)
    (x : R) (hxMem : x ‚àà IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
    (hxNotSq : x ‚àâ (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ^ 2) :
    ‚àÉ _ : Nontrivial (R ‚ß∏ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)),
    ‚àÉ _ : IsLocalRing (R ‚ß∏ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)),
    ‚àÉ _ : IsRegularLocalRing (R ‚ß∏ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)),
      (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (R ‚ß∏ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R))).spanFinrank = k := by
  -- Step 1: instances on R/(x).
  have hxNonunit : ¬¨ IsUnit x := fun hu =>
    (IsLocalRing.notMem_maximalIdeal.mpr hu) hxMem
  have hspan_ne_top : (Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) ‚âÝ ‚ä§ :=
    Ideal.span_singleton_ne_top hxNonunit
  haveI hNT : Nontrivial (R ‚ß∏ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) :=
    Ideal.Quotient.nontrivial_iff.mpr hspan_ne_top
  haveI hLR : IsLocalRing (R ‚ß∏ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) :=
    IsLocalRing.of_surjective' (Ideal.Quotient.mk _) Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
  -- Step 2: cotangent dim drop ‚Üí spanFinrank drop on R/(x).
  have hcot := finrank_cotangentSpace_quot_span_singleton_succ x hxMem hxNotSq
  have hR_cot_eq :
      Module.finrank (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R)
          (IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace R) = k + 1 := by
    rw [‚Üê IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace R]
    exact hdim
  have hR'_cot_eq :
      Module.finrank (IsLocalRing.ResidueField (R ‚ß∏ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)))
          (IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace (R ‚ß∏ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R))) = k := by
    have h := hcot
    rw [hR_cot_eq] at h
    omega
  have hspan_R'_eq_k :
      (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (R ‚ß∏ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R))).spanFinrank = k := by
    rw [IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace
        (R ‚ß∏ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R))]
    exact hR'_cot_eq
  -- Step 3: Krull height theorem: ringKrullDim R ‚â§ ringKrullDim R/(x) + 1.
  have hxJac : x ‚àà Ring.jacobson R := by
    rw [IsLocalRing.ringJacobson_eq_maximalIdeal]
    exact hxMem
  have h_x_subset_jac : ({x} : Set R) ‚äÜ Ring.jacobson R := by
    intro y hy
    rcases hy with rfl
    exact hxJac
  have hKrullDimLE : ringKrullDim R ‚â§
      ringKrullDim (R ‚ß∏ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) + 1 := by
    have h := ringKrullDim_le_ringKrullDim_quotient_add_encard
                ({x} : Set R) h_x_subset_jac
    simpa using h
  have hR_dim : ringKrullDim R = ((k + 1 : ‚Ñï) : WithBot ‚Ñï‚àû) := by
    have h := IsRegularLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal (R := R)
    rw [hdim] at h
    exact_mod_cast h.symm
  -- Step 4: extract `ringKrullDim R/(x) = k` from hKrullDimLE + upper bound.
  -- Upper bound: `ringKrullDim R/(x) ‚â§ spanFinrank ùî™' = k`.
  have h_dim_upper : ringKrullDim (R ‚ß∏ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) ‚â§
      ((k : ‚Ñï) : WithBot ‚Ñï‚àû) := by
    refine le_trans (ringKrullDim_le_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal _) ?_
    rw [hspan_R'_eq_k]
  -- Adding 1 to both sides of h_dim_upper:
  -- `ringKrullDim R/(x) + 1 ‚â§ (k:WithBot) + 1 = (k+1:WithBot) = ringKrullDim R`.
  have h_add_le : ringKrullDim (R ‚ß∏ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) + 1 ‚â§
      ((k + 1 : ‚Ñï) : WithBot ‚Ñï‚àû) := by
    calc ringKrullDim (R ‚ß∏ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) + 1
        ‚â§ ((k : ‚Ñï) : WithBot ‚Ñï‚àû) + 1 := by gcongr
      _ = ((k + 1 : ‚Ñï) : WithBot ‚Ñï‚àû) := by push_cast; ring
  -- Combined with hKrullDimLE via hR_dim ‚Üí equation in WithBot.
  have h_add_eq : ringKrullDim (R ‚ß∏ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) + 1
      = ((k + 1 : ‚Ñï) : WithBot ‚Ñï‚àû) := by
    rw [hR_dim] at hKrullDimLE
    exact le_antisymm h_add_le hKrullDimLE
  -- Extract `ringKrullDim R/(x) = k : WithBot ‚Ñï‚àû` via `WithBot.add_eq_coe`.
  have hR'_dim_eq : ringKrullDim (R ‚ß∏ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R))
      = ((k : ‚Ñï) : WithBot ‚Ñï‚àû) := by
    obtain ‚ü®a', b', ha', hb', hab‚ü© := WithBot.add_eq_coe.mp h_add_eq
    rw [‚Üê ha']
    have hb_eq : b' = (1 : ‚Ñï‚àû) := by
      have h1 : ((b' : ‚Ñï‚àû) : WithBot ‚Ñï‚àû) = ((1 : ‚Ñï‚àû) : WithBot ‚Ñï‚àû) := by
        rw [hb']; simp
      exact_mod_cast h1
    have ha_eq : a' = (k : ‚Ñï‚àû) := by
      rw [hb_eq] at hab
      have hcast2 : a' + 1 = (k : ‚Ñï‚àû) + 1 := by exact_mod_cast hab
      have hne_top : (1 : ‚Ñï‚àû) ‚âÝ ‚ä§ := by simp
      exact WithTop.add_right_cancel hne_top hcast2
    exact_mod_cast ha_eq
  -- Step 5: spanFinrank ùî™' = k = ringKrullDim R/(x) ‚Üí IsRegularLocalRing R/(x).
  have hRLR : IsRegularLocalRing (R ‚ß∏ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) := by
    apply IsRegularLocalRing.of_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_le
    rw [hspan_R'_eq_k, hR'_dim_eq]
  exact ‚ü®hNT, hLR, hRLR, hspan_R'_eq_k‚ü©

  -- Strong induction on `spanFinrank ùî™`, generalising `R` so the IH applies
  -- to the quotient `R/(x)` at smaller dim.
  suffices haux : ‚àÄ (n : ‚Ñï) (R : Type u) [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
      [IsNoetherianRing R] [IsRegularLocalRing R],
      (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = n ‚Üí IsDomain R by
    exact haux _ R rfl
  intro n
  induction n with
  | zero =>
    intros R _ _ _ _ hdim
    exact isDomain_of_isLocalRing_of_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_zero hdim
  | succ k ih =>
    intros R _ _ _ _ hdim
    -- Step 1: pick `x ‚àà ùî™ \ ùî™¬≤` via Nakayama.
    have hpos : 0 < (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank := by omega
    obtain ‚ü®x, hxMem, hxNotSq‚ü© := exists_notMemSq_of_spanFinrank_pos hpos
    -- Step 2: instances + IsRegularLocalRing on R/(x) via the axiom-clean helper.
    obtain ‚ü®hNT, hLR, hRLR, hdim_quot‚ü© :=
      regularLocal_quotient_isRegularLocal_of_notMemSq hdim x hxMem hxNotSq
    -- Step 3: IH on R/(x) at spanFinrank = k.
    have hDomain_R' : IsDomain (R ‚ß∏ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) :=
      ih (R ‚ß∏ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) hdim_quot
    -- Step 4: (x) is prime in R (R/(x) is a domain).

      (Ideal.Quotient.isDomain_iff_prime _).mp hDomain_R'

    obtain ‚ü®ùî≠, hùî≠_min, hùî≠_le‚ü© := Ideal.exists_minimalPrimes_le
      (I := (‚ä• : Ideal R)) (J := Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) bot_le
    haveI hùî≠_prime : ùî≠.IsPrime := Ideal.minimalPrimes_isPrime hùî≠_min
    -- Step 6: case split on x ‚àà ùî≠ vs x ‚àâ ùî≠.

      -- helper, with its narrow typed sorry the only remaining gap.
      have hùî≠_eq : ùî≠ = Ideal.span ({x} : Set R) := by

      exact absurd hmin

    ¬∑ -- Case `x ‚àâ ùî≠`: `ùî≠ ‚äÜ ùî™¬∑ùî≠` by the `y = x¬∑z, z ‚àà ùî≠` argument; Nakayama

      -- is a prime ideal of `R`, hence `IsDomain R`.

   `spanFinrank ùî™ (R/(x)) = k`.
4. Krull dim drop via
   `ringKrullDim_quotient_span_singleton_succ_eq_ringKrullDim`: since `x` is
   `R`-regular and `x ‚àà ùî™`,
   `ringKrullDim (R/(x)) + 1 = ringKrullDim R`. By `IsRegularLocalRing`'s
   defining equation `ringKrullDim R = spanFinrank ùî™ R = k+1`, so
   `ringKrullDim (R/(x)) = k`.
5. Conclude `IsRegularLocalRing (R/(x))` via
   `IsRegularLocalRing.of_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_le` (the inequality
   becomes the equation `spanFinrank = k = ringKrullDim`). -/
private lemma exists_isSMulRegular_quotient_isRegularLocal_succ
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
    [IsRegularLocalRing R] {k : ‚Ñï}
    (hdim : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = k + 1) :
    ‚àÉ (x : R), x ‚àà IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R ‚àß IsSMulRegular R x ‚àß
      ‚àÉ _ : IsRegularLocalRing (R ‚ß∏ Ideal.span {x}),
        (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (R ‚ß∏ Ideal.span {x})).spanFinrank = k := by
  -- Step 1: extract `x ‚àà ùî™ \ ùî™¬≤` that is `R`-regular from the Stacks 00NQ helper.
  obtain ‚ü®x, hxMem, hxNotSq, hxReg‚ü© :=
    exists_isSMulRegular_notMemSq_of_regularLocal_succ (k := k) hdim
  refine ‚ü®x, hxMem, hxReg, ?_‚ü©
  -- Step 2: assemble the structural instances on `R/(x)`.
  have hxNonunit : ¬¨ IsUnit x := fun hu =>
    (IsLocalRing.notMem_maximalIdeal.mpr hu) hxMem
  have hspan_ne_top : (Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) ‚âÝ ‚ä§ :=
    Ideal.span_singleton_ne_top hxNonunit
  haveI : Nontrivial (R ‚ß∏ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) :=
    Ideal.Quotient.nontrivial_iff.mpr hspan_ne_top
  haveI : IsLocalRing (R ‚ß∏ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) :=
    IsLocalRing.of_surjective' (Ideal.Quotient.mk _) Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
  -- IsNoetherianRing (R ‚ß∏ I) is automatic via `Ideal.Quotient.isNoetherianRing`.
  -- Step 3: cotangent dim-drop via Lane G1 helper.
  have hcot := finrank_cotangentSpace_quot_span_singleton_succ x hxMem hxNotSq
  -- Step 4: translate Œ∫-finrank to spanFinrank on both R and R/(x).
  have hR_cot_eq :
      Module.finrank (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R) (IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace R) = k + 1 := by
    rw [‚Üê IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace R]
    exact hdim
  have hR'_cot_eq :
      Module.finrank (IsLocalRing.ResidueField (R ‚ß∏ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)))
          (IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace (R ‚ß∏ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R))) = k := by
    -- from `hcot : LHS + 1 = RHS` and `hR_cot_eq : RHS = k + 1` we get `LHS = k`.
    have h := hcot
    rw [hR_cot_eq] at h
    omega
  have hspan_R'_eq_k :
      (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (R ‚ß∏ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R))).spanFinrank = k := by
    rw [IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace
        (R ‚ß∏ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R))]
    exact hR'_cot_eq
  -- Step 5: Krull dim drop on R/(x).  `ringKrullDim (R/(x)) + 1 = ringKrullDim R`.
  have hKrullDimDrop : ringKrullDim (R ‚ß∏ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) + 1 = ringKrullDim R :=
    ringKrullDim_quotient_span_singleton_succ_eq_ringKrullDim hxReg hxMem
  -- `ringKrullDim R = (k+1 : ‚Ñï)` by `IsRegularLocalRing`'s defining equation.
  have hR_dim : ringKrullDim R = ((k + 1 : ‚Ñï) : WithBot ‚Ñï‚àû) := by
    have h := IsRegularLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal (R := R)
    rw [hdim] at h
    -- h : ((k+1 : ‚Ñï) : WithBot ‚Ñï‚àû) = ringKrullDim R (after coercion through ‚Ñï‚àû)
    exact_mod_cast h.symm
  -- Solve `ringKrullDim (R/(x)) = (k : ‚Ñï)` from the additive equation.
  have hR'_dim : ringKrullDim (R ‚ß∏ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) = ((k : ‚Ñï) : WithBot ‚Ñï‚àû) := by
    rw [hR_dim] at hKrullDimDrop
    -- hKrullDimDrop : ringKrullDim (R/(x)) + 1 = ((k+1 : ‚Ñï) : WithBot ‚Ñï‚àû).
    -- Use `WithBot.add_eq_coe` to extract finite witnesses `a', b' : ‚Ñï‚àû`, then
    -- cancel `+ 1` in `‚Ñï‚àû` via `WithTop.add_right_cancel` (since `1 ‚âÝ ‚ä§`).
    obtain ‚ü®a', b', ha', hb', hab‚ü© := WithBot.add_eq_coe.mp hKrullDimDrop
    rw [‚Üê ha']
    have hb_eq : b' = (1 : ‚Ñï‚àû) := by
      have h1 : ((b' : ‚Ñï‚àû) : WithBot ‚Ñï‚àû) = ((1 : ‚Ñï‚àû) : WithBot ‚Ñï‚àû) := by
        rw [hb']; simp
      exact_mod_cast h1
    have ha_eq : a' = (k : ‚Ñï‚àû) := by
      rw [hb_eq] at hab
      have hcast2 : a' + 1 = (k : ‚Ñï‚àû) + 1 := by exact_mod_cast hab
      have hne_top : (1 : ‚Ñï‚àû) ‚âÝ ‚ä§ := by simp
      exact WithTop.add_right_cancel hne_top hcast2
    exact_mod_cast ha_eq
  -- Step 6: conclude `IsRegularLocalRing (R/(x))` via `of_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_le`.
  have hRegLR : IsRegularLocalRing (R ‚ß∏ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) :=
    IsRegularLocalRing.of_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_le _ <| by
      rw [hspan_R'_eq_k, hR'_dim]
  exact ‚ü®hRegLR, hspan_R'_eq_k‚ü©

/-- **Lane G inductive step (iter-185 structural scaffold).** Packages the
inductive step of Stacks 00OD: given a regular local ring `R` of dimension
`k + 1`, plus the inductive hypothesis at dimension `k` (universally
quantified in the ring), produce a regular sequence of length `k + 1` in
the maximal ideal of `R`.

The body is **axiom-clean** modulo the typed-`sorry` substrate helper
`exists_isSMulRegular_quotient_isRegularLocal_succ`. Assembly path:

1. Helper extracts `x ‚àà ùî™` with `IsSMulRegular R x` AND `IsRegularLocalRing
   (R‚ß∏(x))` of `spanFinrank = k`.
2. IH applied on `R ‚ß∏ Ideal.span {x}` produces a regular sequence
   `rs'_q : List (R ‚ß∏ (x))` of length `k` in the maximal ideal there.
3. Lift `rs'_q` to `rs : List R` via `Function.surjInv` of
   `Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective`; the section property gives
   `rs.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk _) = rs'_q`.
4. Members of `rs` lie in `ùî™` because the maximal ideal of `R‚ß∏(x)` is the
   image of `ùî™` (`IsLocalRing.le_maximalIdeal` applied to the comap chain).
5. Cons via `RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular.cons'` to form the length-`(k+1)`
   sequence `x :: rs`.

This factoring isolates the Mathlib gap to one narrow typed `sorry` instead of
spreading it across the lemma body; future iterations can attack Stacks 00NQ
+ 00NU directly. -/
private lemma regularLocal_inductive_step {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
    [IsNoetherianRing R] [IsRegularLocalRing R] {k : ‚Ñï}
    (hdim : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = k + 1)
    (IH : ‚àÄ (R' : Type u) [CommRing R'] [IsLocalRing R'] [IsNoetherianRing R']
            [IsRegularLocalRing R'],
            (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R').spanFinrank = k ‚Üí
            ‚àÉ rs : List R', rs.length = k ‚àß
              (‚àÄ r ‚àà rs, r ‚àà IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R') ‚àß
              RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular R' rs) :
    ‚àÉ rs : List R, rs.length = k + 1 ‚àß
      (‚àÄ r ‚àà rs, r ‚àà IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ‚àß
      RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular R rs := by
  -- Step 1: extract `x ‚àà ùî™` regular on `R` with `R ‚ß∏ (x)` regular local of
  -- `spanFinrank = k`.  This is the consolidated Stacks 00NQ + 00NU substrate.
  obtain ‚ü®x, hxMem, hxReg, hRLR, hdim_quot‚ü© :=
    exists_isSMulRegular_quotient_isRegularLocal_succ hdim
  -- Step 2: apply IH on `R ‚ß∏ (x)` ‚Äî this gives a regular sequence of length
  -- `k` on the quotient, valued in its maximal ideal.
  obtain ‚ü®rs'_q, hlen_q, hmem_q, hreg_q‚ü© := IH (R ‚ß∏ Ideal.span {x}) hdim_quot
  -- Step 3: lift `rs'_q : List (R ‚ß∏ (x))` to `rs' : List R` via the right
  -- inverse of the (surjective) quotient ring hom.
  let mkq : R ‚Üí+* R ‚ß∏ Ideal.span {x} := Ideal.Quotient.mk _
  let g : R ‚ß∏ Ideal.span {x} ‚Üí R := Function.surjInv Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
  have hg : ‚àÄ y, mkq (g y) = y := Function.surjInv_eq _
  let rs' : List R := rs'_q.map g
  have hlen_rs' : rs'.length = k := by simp [rs', hlen_q]
  have hmkmap : rs'.map mkq = rs'_q := by
    change (rs'_q.map g).map mkq = rs'_q
    rw [List.map_map]
    conv_rhs => rw [‚Üê List.map_id rs'_q]
    exact List.map_congr_left fun y _ => hg y
  -- Step 4: each element of `rs'` lies in `ùî™ R` via the comap of `ùî™ (R‚ß∏(x))`.
  -- The maximal ideal of `R ‚ß∏ (x)` comaps back to `ùî™ R` (it's the *unique*
  -- maximal ideal of `R` containing `Ideal.span {x} ‚äÜ ùî™`).
  have hmem_rs' : ‚àÄ r ‚àà rs', r ‚àà IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := by
    intro r hr
    simp only [rs', List.mem_map] at hr
    obtain ‚ü®y, hy_mem, rfl‚ü© := hr
    -- The comap of `ùî™ (R‚ß∏(x))` under the surjective `mkq` is a maximal ideal
    -- of `R`; since `R` is local, it equals `ùî™ R`.  Hence `g y ‚àà comap mkq ùî™'`
    -- iff `g y ‚àà ùî™ R`.
    have hmax_comap : (Ideal.comap mkq
        (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (R ‚ß∏ Ideal.span {x}))).IsMaximal :=
      Ideal.comap_isMaximal_of_surjective _ Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
    have heq : Ideal.comap mkq
        (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (R ‚ß∏ Ideal.span {x}))
        = IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R :=
      (IsLocalRing.isMaximal_iff R).mp hmax_comap
    rw [‚Üê heq, Ideal.mem_comap]
    exact (hg y).symm ‚ñ∏ hmem_q y hy_mem
  -- Step 5: cons `x` onto `rs'` to form the length-`(k+1)` regular sequence.
  refine ‚ü®x :: rs', ?_, ?_, ?_‚ü©
  ¬∑ simp [hlen_rs']
  ¬∑ intro r hr
    rcases List.mem_cons.mp hr with rfl | hr_in
    ¬∑ exact hxMem
    ¬∑ exact hmem_rs' r hr_in
  ¬∑ -- `IsRegular R (x :: rs')` via `IsRegular.cons'`.
    -- Need `IsSMulRegular R x` (have `hxReg`) AND
    -- `IsRegular (QuotSMulTop x R) (rs'.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {x})))`.
    refine RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular.cons' hxReg ?_
    -- After `cons'`: goal is `IsRegular (QuotSMulTop x R) (rs'.map mkq)`
    -- = `IsRegular (QuotSMulTop x R) rs'_q` (by `hmkmap`), implicit ring
    -- `R ‚ß∏ Ideal.span {x}` (inferred from list type).
    rw [hmkmap]
    -- Goal: `IsRegular (QuotSMulTop x R) rs'_q` (implicit ring `R ‚ß∏ (x)`).
    -- IH provides: `IsRegular (R ‚ß∏ Ideal.span {x}) rs'_q` (same implicit
    -- ring, but M differs: `R ‚ß∏ Ideal.span {x}` vs `QuotSMulTop x R = R ‚ß∏ (x ‚Ä¢ ‚ä§)`).
    --
    -- **iter-185 typed `sorry` ‚Äî TECHNICAL BRIDGE (NOT a Mathlib substrate gap).**
    -- The two M's are *equal as sets* ‚Äî both are the quotient of `R` by the
    -- principal ideal `(x)`, written two different ways:
    --   * `R ‚ß∏ Ideal.span {x}` ‚Äî quotient by `Ideal.span {x}` as a Submodule R R;
    --   * `QuotSMulTop x R = R ‚ß∏ (x ‚Ä¢ ‚ä§)` ‚Äî quotient by `x ‚Ä¢ ‚ä§` as a Submodule R R.
    -- Mathlib's `Submodule.ideal_span_singleton_smul` gives
    -- `(x ‚Ä¢ ‚ä§ : Submodule R R) = Ideal.span {x} ‚Ä¢ ‚ä§ = Ideal.span {x}` (the
    -- second equality because `I ‚Ä¢ ‚ä§_R = I` for an ideal `I` of `R`).  The
    -- bridge between the two M's is an `R‚ß∏(x)`-linear equivalence; future iter
    -- can construct this in ~10-20 LOC via:
    --   (a) `Submodule.quotEquivOfEq` from the submodule equality (gives R-linear);
    --   (b) upgrade to `R‚ß∏(x)`-linear via `QuotSMulTop.mem_annihilator`
    --       (since `x ‚àà Module.annihilator R (QuotSMulTop x R)`, the R-action
    --       factors through `R‚ß∏(x)`, and any R-linear equiv between two such
    --       modules is automatically `R‚ß∏(x)`-linear);
    --   (c) close via `LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr` on this `R‚ß∏(x)`-linear equiv.
    -- This bridge has no substrate dependencies ‚Äî it's pure bookkeeping.
    --
    -- iter-186 Lane G: closed kernel-clean via the explicit `R‚ß∏(x)`-linear
    -- equiv between the two quotients, then `LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr`
    -- transports `hreg_q` across.  The two `mapQ` halves use `LinearMap.id`
    -- with `heq.le` / `heq.ge`, and `map_smul'` reduces to `rfl` after
    -- `Quotient.inductionOn` on the scalar (the `R‚ß∏(x)`-action on both sides
    -- is `[s] ‚Ä¢ [r] = [s * r]` definitionally).
    open scoped Pointwise in
    have heq : (x ‚Ä¢ (‚ä§ : Submodule R R)) = (Ideal.span {x} : Submodule R R) := by
      ext y
      simp [Submodule.mem_smul_pointwise_iff_exists, Ideal.mem_span_singleton,
        eq_comm, Dvd.dvd]
    let e : (R ‚ß∏ (x ‚Ä¢ (‚ä§ : Submodule R R))) ‚âÉ‚Çó[R ‚ß∏ Ideal.span {x}]
        (R ‚ß∏ Ideal.span {x}) := {
      toFun := Submodule.mapQ _ _ LinearMap.id heq.le
      invFun := Submodule.mapQ _ _ LinearMap.id heq.ge
      left_inv := by rintro ‚ü®r‚ü©; rfl
      right_inv := by rintro ‚ü®r‚ü©; rfl
      map_add' := map_add _
      map_smul' := by
        rintro q ‚ü®r‚ü©
        induction q using Quotient.inductionOn with
        | _ s => rfl
    }
    exact (LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr e.symm rs'_q).mp hreg_q

lemma exists_isRegular_of_regularLocal
    (R : Type u) [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
    [IsRegularLocalRing R] :
    ‚àÉ rs : List R, rs.length = (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank
        ‚àß (‚àÄ r ‚àà rs, r ‚àà IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
        ‚àß RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular R rs := by
  -- Strong induction on `n = spanFinrank R`, generalising `R` so the inductive
  -- hypothesis can be applied to the quotient `R/(x)` at smaller dimension.
  suffices haux : ‚àÄ (n : ‚Ñï) (R : Type u) [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
      [IsNoetherianRing R] [IsRegularLocalRing R],
      (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = n ‚Üí
      ‚àÉ rs : List R, rs.length = n ‚àß
        (‚àÄ r ‚àà rs, r ‚àà IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ‚àß
        RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular R rs by
    exact haux _ R rfl
  intro n
  induction n with
  | zero =>
    -- Base case `dim 0`: spanFinrank = 0, hence `maximalIdeal R = ‚ä•` (so `R` is
    -- a field). The empty list is trivially `R`-regular on the nonzero ring `R`.
    intros R _ _ _ _ _hdim
    refine ‚ü®[], rfl, by simp, ?_‚ü©
    exact RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular.nil R R
  | succ k ih =>
    -- Inductive case `dim (k + 1)`: delegate to `regularLocal_inductive_step`,
    -- supplying the inductive hypothesis at dimension `k`. The helper handles
    -- the substantive NZD-extraction + quotient-regularity + cons assembly.
    intros R _ _ _ _ hdim
    exact regularLocal_inductive_step (k := k) hdim (fun R' _ _ _ _ h => ih R' h)

/-- **Regular local rings are Cohen‚ÄìMacaulay.** Every regular Noetherian
local ring is Cohen‚ÄìMacaulay: a minimal generating set of `ùî™` is an
`R`-regular sequence of length `dim R`, so `depth(R) ‚â• dim R`; combined
with the standard upper bound `depth(R) ‚â§ dim R` (Stacks 00LK) this gives
`depth(R) = dim R`.

This is the consumer-facing input for **A.4.a** (codim-1 extension of a
rational map across a codim-2 closed point on a regular projective
surface): the local ring `O_{S,x}` of a regular projective surface at a
closed point is regular of Krull dimension `2`, hence Cohen‚ÄìMacaulay,
hence has depth `2`, which is exactly the input the local-cohomology
vanishing `H^i_x(O_S) = 0` for `i < 2` needs (Stacks 0AVF; see Hartshorne
III.7).

iter-181 Lane G **STRUCTURAL**: body is now decomposed into two
typed helper lemmas ‚Äî `length_le_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular` (the upper
bound, closed kernel-clean from
`ringKrullDim_add_length_eq_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular`) and
`exists_isRegular_of_regularLocal` (the lower bound, typed `sorry` on the
Mathlib gap `IsRegularLocalRing ‚üπ IsDomain` + regular-quotient induction).
The combined assembly into `depth = ringKrullDim` is closed inline below,
so the only residual `sorry` in this `instance` body is the named helper. -/
instance of_regular (R : Type u) [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
    [IsNoetherianRing R] [IsRegularLocalRing R] : CohenMacaulay R where
  depth_eq_krullDim := by
    -- Step 1: simplify `Module.depth` via the `else` branch
    --   (since `ùî™ ‚Ä¢ ‚ä§ = ùî™ ‚âÝ ‚ä§` for a local ring's maximal ideal).
    have hùî™ : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ‚Ä¢ (‚ä§ : Submodule R R)
        ‚âÝ (‚ä§ : Submodule R R) := by
      have heq : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ‚Ä¢ (‚ä§ : Submodule R R)
          = IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := by simp
      rw [heq]
      exact (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal.isMaximal R).ne_top
    rw [Module.depth, if_neg hùî™]
    -- Step 2: convert RHS to the spanFinrank using
    -- `IsRegularLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal`.
    rw [‚Üê IsRegularLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal]
    -- Goal: ((sSup {n | ‚Ä¶} : ‚Ñï‚àû) : WithBot ‚Ñï‚àû)
    --         = ((spanFinrank ùî™ : ‚Ñï) : WithBot ‚Ñï‚àû)
    -- Step 3: it suffices to show the sSup equals spanFinrank as ‚Ñï‚àû,
    -- via antisymmetry: upper bound from Helper 1, lower bound from Helper 2.
    have h1 : (sSup { n : ‚Ñï‚àû | ‚àÉ rs : List R, (rs.length : ‚Ñï‚àû) = n ‚àß
        (‚àÄ r ‚àà rs, r ‚àà IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
          ‚àß RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular R rs }
        : ‚Ñï‚àû) = ((IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank : ‚Ñï‚àû) := by
      apply le_antisymm
      ¬∑ -- Upper bound: every element of the sSup-set is at most spanFinrank.
        apply sSup_le
        rintro n ‚ü®rs, rfl, _, hreg‚ü©
        have hub := length_le_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular hreg
        rw [‚Üê IsRegularLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal] at hub
        exact_mod_cast hub
      ¬∑ -- Lower bound: spanFinrank is achieved by Helper 2's regular sequence.
        obtain ‚ü®rs, hlen, hmem, hreg‚ü© := exists_isRegular_of_regularLocal R
        apply le_sSup
        refine ‚ü®rs, ?_, hmem, hreg‚ü©
        exact_mod_cast hlen
    rw [h1]
    -- Final coercion: `((n : ‚Ñï‚àû) : WithBot ‚Ñï‚àû) = ((n : ‚Ñï) : WithBot ‚Ñï‚àû)`
    -- is the standard `Nat.cast`-tower commutation.
    rfl

end CohenMacaulay
